These columns are missing and required for Flow 2.\n\n```sql\n-- owner: discovery-catalog module\nALTER TABLE venue\n    ADD COLUMN capacity      INTEGER,\n    ADD COLUMN seating_mode  VARCHAR(20) DEFAULT 'GA'\n                             CONSTRAINT chk_venue_seating_mode CHECK (seating_mode IN ('GA', 'RESERVED')),\n    ADD COLUMN sync_status   VARCHAR(20) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'PENDING_SYNC'\n                             CONSTRAINT chk_venue_sync_status CHECK (sync_status IN ('PENDING_SYNC', 'SYNCED', 'DRIFT_FLAGGED')),\n    ADD COLUMN last_sync_error     TEXT,\n    ADD COLUMN last_attempted_at   TIMESTAMP;\n\n-- Backfill: venues that already have an eb_venue_id are already synced.\n-- Without this, the VenueReconciliationJob would treat all pre-existing venues\n-- as un-synced on its first run and attempt unnecessary EB re-syncs.\nUPDATE venue\n    SET sync_status = 'SYNCED'\n    WHERE eventbrite_venue_id IS NOT NULL;\n\nCREATE INDEX idx_venue_sync_status ON venue(sync_status);\n```\n\n---\n\n### V6 — `CREATE TABLE organization_auth` *(shared/eventbrite/ — OAuth token store)*\n\n```sql\n-- owner: shared/eventbrite/ — never accessed directly by any application module\nCREATE TYPE org_auth_status AS ENUM ('PENDING', 'CONNECTED', 'TOKEN_EXPIRED', 'REVOKED');\n\nCREATE TABLE organization_auth (\n    id                        BIGSERIAL PRIMARY KEY,\n    organization_id           BIGINT       NOT NULL UNIQUE,\n    eb_organization_id        VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,\n    access_token_encrypted    TEXT         NOT NULL,\n    refresh_token_encrypted   TEXT,\n    expires_at                TIMESTAMP,\n    status                    org_auth_status NOT NULL DEFAULT 'PENDING',\n    created_at                TIMESTAMP    NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,\n    updated_at                TIMESTAMP    NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,\n\n    CONSTRAINT fk_org_auth_org FOREIGN KEY (organization_id)\n        REFERENCES organization(id) ON DELETE CASCADE\n);\n\nCREATE UNIQUE INDEX uk_org_auth_org      ON organization_auth(organization_id);\nCREATE INDEX        idx_org_auth_status  ON organization_auth(status);\nCREATE INDEX        idx_org_auth_eb_org  ON organization_auth(eb_organization_id);\n```\n\n**Notes:**\n- One row per org — enforced by `UNIQUE` on `organization_id`.\n- `access_token_encrypted` / `refresh_token_encrypted` — stored encrypted at application layer (AES-256). Columns are `TEXT` because ciphertext length is unpredictable.\n- `refresh_token_encrypted` is nullable — not all OAuth grants issue a refresh token.\n- `expires_at` is nullable — some tokens are long-lived without expiry.\n- No module outside `shared/eventbrite/` may read or write this table.\n\n---\n\n### V7 — `CREATE TABLE show_slot` *(scheduling — primary aggregate)*\n\n```sql\n-- owner: scheduling module\nCREATE TYPE show_slot_status AS ENUM ('DRAFT', 'PENDING_SYNC', 'ACTIVE', 'CANCELLED');\nCREATE TYPE seating_mode     AS ENUM ('GA', 'RESERVED');\n\nCREATE TABLE show_slot (\n    id                    BIGSERIAL    PRIMARY KEY,\n    organization_id       BIGINT       NOT NULL,\n    venue_id              BIGINT       NOT NULL,\n    city_id               BIGINT       NOT NULL,\n    title                 VARCHAR(500) NOT NULL,\n    description           TEXT,\n    start_time            TIMESTAMPTZ  NOT NULL,\n    end_time              TIMESTAMPTZ  NOT NULL,\n    seating_mode          seating_mode NOT NULL,\n    capacity              INTEGER      NOT NULL,\n    source_seatmap_id     VARCHAR(255),\n    is_recurring          BOOLEAN      NOT NULL DEFAULT FALSE,\n    recurrence_rule       TEXT,\n    status                show_slot_status NOT NULL DEFAULT 'DRAFT',\n    eb_event_id           VARCHAR(255) UNIQUE,\n    eb_series_id          VARCHAR(255),\n    sync_attempt_count    INTEGER      NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,\n    last_sync_error       TEXT,\n    last_attempted_at     TIMESTAMPTZ,\n    created_at            TIMESTAMPTZ  NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,\n    updated_at            TIMESTAMPTZ  NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,\n\n    CONSTRAINT fk_show_slot_org   FOREIGN KEY (organization_id) REFERENCES organization(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,\n    CONSTRAINT fk_show_slot_venue FOREIGN KEY (venue_id)        REFERENCES venue(id)        ON DELETE RESTRICT,\n    CONSTRAINT fk_show_slot_city  FOREIGN KEY (city_id)         REFERENCES city(id)         ON DELETE RESTRICT,\n    CONSTRAINT chk_slot_times     CHECK (end_time > start_time),\n    CONSTRAINT chk_slot_capacity  CHECK (capacity > 0),\n    CONSTRAINT chk_recurring_rule CHECK (\n        (is_recurring = FALSE AND recurrence_rule IS NULL) OR\n        (is_recurring = TRUE)\n    )\n);\n\nCREATE INDEX idx_show_slot_org_id     ON show_slot(organization_id);\nCREATE INDEX idx_show_slot_venue_id   ON show_slot(venue_id);\nCREATE INDEX idx_show_slot_city_id    ON show_slot(city_id);\nCREATE INDEX idx_show_slot_status     ON show_slot(status);\nCREATE INDEX idx_show_slot_eb_event   ON show_slot(eb_event_id);\n-- Conflict detection query index: venue + time range overlap\nCREATE INDEX idx_show_slot_venue_time ON show_slot(venue_id, start_time, end_time);\n-- Cache invalidation + reconciliation job: ACTIVE slots with eb_event_id, grouped by city\nCREATE INDEX idx_show_slot_active_eb  ON show_slot(status, eb_event_id) WHERE status = 'ACTIVE';\nCREATE INDEX idx_show_slot_org_city   ON show_slot(organization_id, city_id);\n```\n\n**Notes:**\n- `venue_id` is a scalar FK to `venue.id` within the same DB — allowed because both tables exist. No `@ManyToOne` **JPA import** across module packages.\n- `TIMESTAMPTZ` (timestamp with time zone) used for all time fields — venues span cities and timezones.\n- `ON DELETE RESTRICT` on `venue_id` — a venue with active slots cannot be deleted without first cancelling slots.\n- `source_seatmap_id` stores the Eventbrite seatmap template ID for `RESERVED` slots — nullable, string.\n- `eb_series_id` nullable — only set when `is_recurring = TRUE` and EB schedule creation succeeds.\n- `sync_attempt_count` reset to 0 when slot reaches `ACTIVE`.\n\n---\n\n### V8 — `CREATE TABLE show_slot_occurrence` *(scheduling — recurring child records)*\n\n```sql\n-- owner: scheduling module\nCREATE TABLE show_slot_occurrence (\n    id                    BIGSERIAL   PRIMARY KEY,\n    parent_slot_id        BIGINT      NOT NULL,\n    occurrence_index      INTEGER     NOT NULL,\n    start_time            TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL,\n    end_time              TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL,\n    status                show_slot_status NOT NULL DEFAULT 'ACTIVE',\n    eb_event_id           VARCHAR(255) UNIQUE,\n    sync_attempt_count    INTEGER     NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,\n    last_sync_error       TEXT,\n    last_attempted_at     TIMESTAMPTZ,\n    created_at            TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,\n\n    CONSTRAINT fk_occurrence_slot CHECK (end_time > start_time),\n    CONSTRAINT fk_slot_occurrence FOREIGN KEY (parent_slot_id)\n        REFERENCES show_slot(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,\n    CONSTRAINT uk_occurrence_slot_index UNIQUE (parent_slot_id, occurrence_index)\n);\n\nCREATE INDEX idx_occurrence_parent   ON show_slot_occurrence(parent_slot_id);\nCREATE INDEX idx_occurrence_status   ON show_slot_occurrence(status);\nCREATE INDEX idx_occurrence_eb_event ON show_slot_occurrence(eb_event_id);\n```\n\n**Notes:**\n- `ON DELETE CASCADE` — if the parent `show_slot` is deleted, all occurrences are deleted.\n- `UNIQUE (parent_slot_id, occurrence_index)` — prevents duplicate occurrence positions.\n- `status` is independent per occurrence — not mirrored from parent slot.\n- `eb_event_id` UNIQUE — each occurrence has its own EB-assigned event ID.\n\n---\n\n### V9 — `CREATE TABLE show_slot_pricing_tier` *(scheduling — ticket tier definitions)*\n\n```sql\n-- owner: scheduling module\nCREATE TYPE tier_type AS ENUM ('FREE', 'PAID', 'DONATION');\n\nCREATE TABLE show_slot_pricing_tier (\n    id                    BIGSERIAL    PRIMARY KEY,\n    slot_id               BIGINT       NOT NULL,\n    name                  VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,\n    price_amount          DECIMAL(12,2) NOT NULL DEFAULT 0.00,\n    currency              VARCHAR(3)   NOT NULL DEFAULT 'INR',\n    quota                 INTEGER      NOT NULL,\n    tier_type             tier_type    NOT NULL DEFAULT 'PAID',\n    eb_ticket_class_id    VARCHAR(255),\n    eb_inventory_tier_id  VARCHAR(255),\n    created_at            TIMESTAMPTZ  NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,\n    updated_at            TIMESTAMPTZ  NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,\n\n    CONSTRAINT fk_pricing_tier_slot FOREIGN KEY (slot_id)\n        REFERENCES show_slot(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,\n    CONSTRAINT chk_quota_positive   CHECK (quota > 0),\n    CONSTRAINT chk_price_free       CHECK (\n        (tier_type = 'FREE' AND price_amount = 0.00) OR\n        (tier_type != 'FREE')\n    )\n);\n\nCREATE INDEX idx_pricing_tier_slot   ON show_slot_pricing_tier(slot_id);\nCREATE INDEX idx_pricing_tier_eb     ON show_slot_pricing_tier(eb_ticket_class_id);\n```\n\n**Notes:**\n- `ON DELETE CASCADE` — deleting a `show_slot` removes all its pricing tiers.\n- `eb_ticket_class_id` — populated in Step D.2 when `EbTicketService.createTicketClasses()` returns.\n- `eb_inventory_tier_id` — populated in Step D.3 when `EbTicketService.createInventoryTiers()` returns.\n- `DECIMAL(12,2)` — supports up to ₹9,999,999,999.99; adequate for INR event pricing.\n- `chk_price_free` — enforces that `FREE` tier must have `price_amount = 0`. `PAID` and `DONATION` tiers can have any non-negative amount.\n- `currency` — ISO 4217 3-char code; NOT a FK — kept as string for `scheduling` |\n| `ShowSlotOccurrence` | `show_slot_occurrence` | `scheduling` |\n| `ShowSlotPricingTier` | `show_slot_pricing_tier` | `scheduling` |\n\n\n---\n\n## Stage 5 — API Endpoints\n\n> **Path conventions (corrected from outline, based on existing Flow 1 code):**\n> - Public catalog reads (Flow 1 existing): `GET /api/v1/catalog/...`\n> - Admin writes for catalog/venues (Flow 2 new): `POST|PUT /api/v1/admin/catalog/...` — `ROLE_ADMIN` only\n> - Show slot operations: `/api/v1/scheduling/slots/...` — `ROLE_ADMIN` for writes, `ROLE_ADMIN` for reads\n> - Org OAuth onboarding: `/api/v1/admin/orgs/.../eventbrite/...` — `ROLE_ADMIN` only\n> - Admin dashboard aggregations: `/api/v1/admin/...` — `ROLE_ADMIN` only\n\n---\n\n### 1. Org OAuth Onboarding — `admin/` module\n\n---\n\n#### `POST /api/v1/admin/orgs/{orgId}/eventbrite/connect`\n\nInitiates the Eventbrite OAuth flow for an org. Returns the EB authorization URL for the admin to redirect to.\n\n**Auth:** `ROLE_ADMIN`  \n**Path params:** `orgId` (Long)  \n**Request body:** none  \n**Response `200`:**\n```json\n{\n  \"authorizationUrl\": \"https://www.eventbrite.com/oauth/authorize?response_type=code&client_id=...&redirect_uri=...&state=...\"\n}\n```\n**Response `409`:** org already has a `CONNECTED` token — return current status  \n**Notes:**\n- Generates a CSRF `state` parameter stored in session or short-lived Redis key.\n- Does NOT create an `organization_auth` row yet — that happens on callback.\n\n---\n\n#### `GET /api/v1/admin/orgs/{orgId}/eventbrite/callback`\n\nOAuth callback endpoint. Receives `code` + `state` from Eventbrite, exchanges for token, persists `organization_auth`.\n\n**Auth:** none (public callback URL — `state` param is the CSRF guard)  \n**Query params:** `code` (String), `state` (String)  \n**Response `302`:** redirect to admin dashboard on success  \n**Response `400`:** invalid or expired `state`  \n**Response `502`:** EB token exchange failed  \n**Notes:**\n- Validates `state` against stored value before proceeding.\n- Stores `access_token_encrypted`, `refresh_token_encrypted`, `eb_organization_id`, `expires_at` in `organization_auth`.\n- Sets `status = CONNECTED`.\n\n---\n\n#### `GET /api/v1/admin/orgs/{orgId}/eventbrite/status`\n\nReturns the current OAuth token status for an org.\n\n**Auth:** `ROLE_ADMIN`  \n"
  }
}
```

## Entry 86
**Timestamp:** 2026-03-03T09:49:09.841Z
**Role/Type:** response_item
**Content:**
```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-03T09:49:09.841Z",
  "type": "response_item",
  "payload": {
    "type": "function_call_output",
    "call_id": "call_VpA6O6Wq9PBr3ozbBBJ0z5kg",
    "output": "Chunk ID: 9873e8\nWall time: 0.0510 seconds\nProcess exited with code 0\nOriginal token count: 1596\nOutput:\npackage com.eventplatform.discoverycatalog.service;\n\nimport com.eventplatform.discoverycatalog.domain.EventCatalogItem;\nimport com.eventplatform.discoverycatalog.domain.enums.EventSource;\nimport com.eventplatform.discoverycatalog.domain.enums.EventState;\nimport com.eventplatform.discoverycatalog.exception.CatalogSyncException;\nimport com.eventplatform.discoverycatalog.repository.EventCatalogRepository;\nimport com.eventplatform.discoverycatalog.repository.VenueRepository;\nimport com.eventplatform.discoverycatalog.domain.Venue;\nimport com.eventplatform.shared.eventbrite.dto.EbEventDto;\nimport com.eventplatform.shared.eventbrite.exception.EbAuthException;\nimport com.eventplatform.shared.eventbrite.exception.EbIntegrationException;\nimport com.eventplatform.shared.eventbrite.service.EbEventSyncService;\nimport java.time.Instant;\nimport java.util.HashMap;\nimport java.util.List;\nimport java.util.Map;\nimport java.util.Set;\nimport org.slf4j.Logger;\nimport org.slf4j.LoggerFactory;\nimport org.springframework.stereotype.Service;\nimport org.springframework.transaction.annotation.Transactional;\n\n@Service\npublic class EventCatalogSyncService {\n\n    private static final Logger log = LoggerFactory.getLogger(EventCatalogSyncService.class);\n\n    private final EbEventSyncService ebEventSyncService;\n    private final EventCatalogRepository eventCatalogRepository;\n    private final VenueRepository venueRepository;\n\n    public EventCatalogSyncService(\n        EbEventSyncService ebEventSyncService,\n        EventCatalogRepository eventCatalogRepository,\n        VenueRepository venueRepository\n    ) {\n        this.ebEventSyncService = ebEventSyncService;\n        this.eventCatalogRepository = eventCatalogRepository;\n        this.venueRepository = venueRepository;\n    }\n\n    @Transactional\n    public void sync(Long organizationId, Long cityId, Long venueId) {\n        List<EbEventDto> ebEvents = fetchEvents(organizationId, venueId);\n        Map<String, EbEventDto> ebEventById = new HashMap<>();\n        for (EbEventDto ebEvent : ebEvents) {\n            ebEventById.put(ebEvent.id(), ebEvent);\n        }\n\n        for (EbEventDto ebEvent : ebEvents) {\n            upsertEvent(organizationId, cityId, ebEvent);\n        }\n\n        softDeleteMissing(organizationId, cityId, venueId, ebEventById.keySet());\n    }\n\n    private List<EbEventDto> fetchEvents(Long organizationId, Long venueId) {\n        try {\n            if (venueId != null) {\n                String eventbriteVenueId = venueRepository.findById(venueId)\n                    .map(Venue::getEventbriteVenueId)\n                    .orElse(null);\n                if (eventbriteVenueId == null) {\n                    throw new CatalogSyncException(\"Venue missing eventbrite_venue_id: \" + venueId);\n                }\n                return ebEventSyncService.listEventsByVenue(eventbriteVenueId);\n            }\n            return ebEventSyncService.listEventsByOrganization(organizationId.toString());\n        } catch (EbAuthException ex) {\n            throw ex;\n        } catch (EbIntegrationException ex) {\n            throw ex;\n        } catch (Exception ex) {\n            throw new CatalogSyncException(\"Failed to sync events from Eventbrite\", ex);\n        }\n    }\n\n    private void upsertEvent(Long organizationId, Long cityId, EbEventDto ebEvent) {\n        if (ebEvent.venueId() == null) {\n            log.warn(\"Skipping Eventbrite event without venue id. eventId={}\", ebEvent.id());\n            return;\n        }\n        var venueOpt = venueRepository.findByEventbriteVenueId(ebEvent.venueId());\n        if (venueOpt.isEmpty()) {\n            log.warn(\"Skipping Eventbrite event with unknown venue. venueId={}\", ebEvent.venueId());\n            return;\n        }\n        Long resolvedCityId = venueOpt.get().getCityId();\n        if (!resolvedCityId.equals(cityId)) {\n            log.debug(\"Event city mismatch. eventId={} expectedCityId={} actualCityId={}\", ebEvent.id(), cityId, resolvedCityId);\n        }\n        EventCatalogItem incoming = new EventCatalogItem(\n            organizationId,\n            resolvedCityId,\n            ebEvent.id(),\n            ebEvent.name(),\n            ebEvent.startTime(),\n            ebEvent.endTime()\n        );\n        incoming.applyEventbriteDetails(\n            venueOpt.get().getId(),\n            ebEvent.description(),\n            ebEvent.url(),\n            mapState(ebEvent.state()),\n            EventSource.EVENTBRITE_EXTERNAL,\n            ebEvent.currency(),\n            ebEvent.changedAt()\n        );\n\n        eventCatalogRepository.findByEventbriteEventId(ebEvent.id()).ifPresentOrElse(existing -> {\n            if (shouldUpdate(existing, ebEvent.changedAt())) {\n                existing.updateFrom(incoming);\n            }\n        }, () -> eventCatalogRepository.save(incoming));\n    }\n\n    private boolean shouldUpdate(EventCatalogItem existing, Instant incomingChangedAt) {\n        if (incomingChangedAt == null) {\n            return true;\n        }\n        Instant currentChangedAt = existing.getEventbriteChangedAt();\n        return currentChangedAt == null || incomingChangedAt.isAfter(currentChangedAt);\n    }\n\n    private EventState mapState(String state) {\n        if (state == null) {\n            return EventState.PUBLISHED;\n        }\n        return switch (state.toUpperCase()) {\n            case \"DRAFT\" -> EventState.DRAFT;\n            case \"CANCELLED\" -> EventState.CANCELLED;\n            default -> EventState.PUBLISHED;\n        };\n    }\n\n    private void softDeleteMissing(Long organizationId, Long cityId, Long venueId, Set<String> eventbriteIds) {\n        List<EventCatalogItem> existing;\n        if (venueId == null) {\n            existing = eventCatalogRepository.findByOrganizationIdAndCityIdAndDeletedAtIsNull(organizationId, cityId);\n        } else {\n            existing = eventCatalogRepository.findByOrganizationIdAndCityIdAndVenueIdAndDeletedAtIsNull(organizationId, cityId, venueId);\n        }\n        Instant now = Instant.now();\n        List<EventCatalogItem> toDelete = existing.stream()\n            .filter(item -> !eventbriteIds.contains(item.getEventbriteEventId()))\n            .toList();\n        toDelete.forEach(item -> item.softDelete(now));\n        if (!toDelete.isEmpty()) {\n            log.info(\"Soft-deleted {} missing events for orgId={} cityId={} venueId={}\", toDelete.size(), organizationId, cityId, venueId);\n        }\n    }\n}\n"
  }
}
```

com.eventplatform.shared.eventbrite.exception.EbIntegrationException;\nimport com.eventplatform.shared.eventbrite.service.EbEventSyncService;\nimport java.time.Instant;\nimport java.util.HashMap;\nimport java.util.List;\nimport java.util.Map;\nimport java.util.Set;\nimport org.slf4j.Logger;\nimport org.slf4j.LoggerFactory;\nimport org.springframework.stereotype.Service;\nimport org.springframework.transaction.annotation.Transactional;\n\n@Service\npublic class EventCatalogSyncService {\n\n    private static final Logger log = LoggerFactory.getLogger(EventCatalogSyncService.class);\n\n    private final EbEventSyncService ebEventSyncService;\n    private final EventCatalogRepository eventCatalogRepository;\n    private final VenueRepository venueRepository;\n\n    public EventCatalogSyncService(\n        EbEventSyncService ebEventSyncService,\n        EventCatalogRepository eventCatalogRepository,\n        VenueRepository venueRepository\n    ) {\n        this.ebEventSyncService = ebEventSyncService;\n        this.eventCatalogRepository = eventCatalogRepository;\n        this.venueRepository = venueRepository;\n    }\n\n    @Transactional\n    public void sync(Long organizationId, Long cityId, Long venueId) {\n        List<EbEventDto> ebEvents = fetchEvents(organizationId, venueId);\n        Map<String, EbEventDto> ebEventById = new HashMap<>();\n        for (EbEventDto ebEvent : ebEvents) {\n            ebEventById.put(ebEvent.id(), ebEvent);\n        }\n\n        for (EbEventDto ebEvent : ebEvents) {\n            upsertEvent(organizationId, cityId, ebEvent);\n        }\n\n        softDeleteMissing(organizationId, cityId, venueId, ebEventById.keySet());\n    }\n\n    private List<EbEventDto> fetchEvents(Long organizationId, Long venueId) {\n        try {\n            if (venueId != null) {\n                String eventbriteVenueId = venueRepository.findById(venueId)\n                    .map(Venue::getEventbriteVenueId)\n                    .orElse(null);\n                if (eventbriteVenueId == null) {\n                    throw new CatalogSyncException(\"Venue missing eventbrite_venue_id: \" + venueId);\n                }\n                return ebEventSyncService.listEventsByVenue(eventbriteVenueId);\n            }\n            return ebEventSyncService.listEventsByOrganization(organizationId.toString());\n        } catch (EbAuthException ex) {\n            throw ex;\n        } catch (EbIntegrationException ex) {\n            throw ex;\n        } catch (Exception ex) {\n            throw new CatalogSyncException(\"Failed to sync events from Eventbrite\", ex);\n        }\n    }\n\n    private void upsertEvent(Long organizationId, Long cityId, EbEventDto ebEvent) {\n        if (ebEvent.venueId() == null) {\n            log.warn(\"Skipping Eventbrite event without venue id. eventId={}\", ebEvent.id());\n            return;\n        }\n        var venueOpt = venueRepository.findByEventbriteVenueId(ebEvent.venueId());\n        if (venueOpt.isEmpty()) {\n            log.warn(\"Skipping Eventbrite event with unknown venue. venueId={}\", ebEvent.venueId());\n            return;\n        }\n        Long resolvedCityId = venueOpt.get().getCityId();\n        if (!resolvedCityId.equals(cityId)) {\n            log.debug(\"Event city mismatch. eventId={} expectedCityId={} actualCityId={}\", ebEvent.id(), cityId, resolvedCityId);\n        }\n        EventCatalogItem incoming = new EventCatalogItem(\n            organizationId,\n            resolvedCityId,\n            ebEvent.id(),\n            ebEvent.name(),\n            ebEvent.startTime(),\n            ebEvent.endTime()\n        );\n        incoming.applyEventbriteDetails(\n            venueOpt.get().getId(),\n            ebEvent.description(),\n            ebEvent.url(),\n            mapState(ebEvent.state()),\n            EventSource.EVENTBRITE_EXTERNAL,\n            ebEvent.currency(),\n            ebEvent.changedAt()\n        );\n\n        eventCatalogRepository.findByEventbriteEventId(ebEvent.id()).ifPresentOrElse(existing -> {\n            if (shouldUpdate(existing, ebEvent.changedAt())) {\n                existing.updateFrom(incoming);\n            }\n        }, () -> eventCatalogRepository.save(incoming));\n    }\n\n    private boolean shouldUpdate(EventCatalogItem existing, Instant incomingChangedAt) {\n        if (incomingChangedAt == null) {\n            return true;\n        }\n        Instant currentChangedAt = existing.getEventbriteChangedAt();\n        return currentChangedAt == null || incomingChangedAt.isAfter(currentChangedAt);\n    }\n\n    private EventState mapState(String state) {\n        if (state == null) {\n            return EventState.PUBLISHED;\n        }\n        return switch (state.toUpperCase()) {\n            case \"DRAFT\" -> EventState.DRAFT;\n            case \"CANCELLED\" -> EventState.CANCELLED;\n            default -> EventState.PUBLISHED;\n        };\n    }\n\n    private void softDeleteMissing(Long organizationId, Long cityId, Long venueId, Set<String> eventbriteIds) {\n        List<EventCatalogItem> existing;\n        if (venueId == null) {\n            existing = eventCatalogRepository.findByOrganizationIdAndCityIdAndDeletedAtIsNull(organizationId, cityId);\n        } else {\n            existing = eventCatalogRepository.findByOrganizationIdAndCityIdAndVenueIdAndDeletedAtIsNull(organizationId, cityId, venueId);\n        }\n        Instant now = Instant.now();\n        List<EventCatalogItem> toDelete = existing.stream()\n            .filter(item -> !eventbriteIds.contains(item.getEventbriteEventId()))\n            .toList();\n        toDelete.forEach(item -> item.softDelete(now));\n        if (!toDelete.isEmpty()) {\n            log.info(\"Soft-deleted {} missing events for orgId={} cityId={} venueId={}\", toDelete.size(), organizationId, cityId, venueId);\n        }\n    }\n}\n"
  }
}
```

{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-03T09:50:09.982Z",

  "payload": {
    "type": "function_call_output",
    "call_id": "call_9NkFFtWnknLUJfrM3a2InNQA",
    "output": "Chunk ID: 5f9ab4\nWall time: 0.0522 seconds\nProcess exited with code 0\nOriginal token count: 106745\nOutput:\nTotal output lines: 38\n\nagents/SPEC_GENERATOR.md:252:│   │                                            No business logic. @PreAuthorize for role checks.\nagents/SPEC_GENERATOR.md:389:- Auth requirement (none / authenticated user / role-scoped via `@PreAuthorize`)\nagents/SPEC_GENERATOR.md:400:Auth:     none | @PreAuthorize(\"hasRole('USER')\") | @PreAuthorize(\"hasRole('ADMIN')\")\nagents/SPEC_GENERATOR.md:523:- ROLE_ADMIN endpoints must use @PreAuthorize(\"hasRole('ADMIN')\") on the controller method\nagents/SPEC_GENERATOR.md:692:`@PreAuthorize` enforcement. Nothing else.\nagents/SPEC_GENERATOR.md:936:[Who can do what, under what conditions. Mapped to @PreAuthorize annotations.]\nagents/CODING_AGENT.md:443:@PreAuthorize(\"hasRole('USER')\")\nagents/CODING_AGENT.md:513:- Use `@PreAuthorize` for role-based access — never check roles in service or domain\nagents/CODING_AGENT.md:532:    @PreAuthorize(\"hasRole('USER')\")\nagents/CODING_AGENT.md:541:    @PreAuthorize(\"hasRole('USER')\")\nagents/CODING_AGENT.md:551:    @PreAuthorize(\"hasRole('USER')\")\nagents/CODING_AGENT.md:855:@PreAuthorize(\"hasRole('USER')\")\nagents/CODING_AGENT.md:875:- Method-level security uses `@PreAuthorize` on controller methods — never on service methods\nagents/CODING_AGENT.md:877:- Never hardcode role strings outside of `@PreAuthorize` annotations — define constants in `shared/security/Roles`\nagents/CODING_AGENT.md:881:@PreAuthorize(\"hasRole('\" + Roles.ADMIN + \"')\")\nagents/CODING_AGENT.md:884:@PreAuthorize(\"hasRole('ADMIN')\")\nagents/CODING_AGENT.md:953:@PreAuthorize(\"hasRole('USER')\")\nagents/CODING_AGENT.md:1031:- [ ] **`@PreAuthorize` on controller methods only — never on service methods**\ndocs/MODULE_STRUCTURE_TEMPLATE.md:171:    ├── api/controller/            ← Admin-only endpoints (@PreAuthorize ROLE_ADMIN)\ndocs/TESTING_GUIDE.md:441:validation enforcement, and `@PreAuthorize` role checks. Nothing else.\nPOQ/Fake_Service/Fake_Seervice_TestSessions.md:1382:    \"output\": \"Chunk ID: 7b44dd\\nWall time: 0.0517 seconds\\nProcess exited with code 0\\nOriginal token count: 3366\\nOutput:\\n# Modular Monolith — Canonical Folder Structure Template\\n\\n> **Purpose:** This document is the canonical reference for building any new feature or module\\n> in this codebase. Every agent, developer, or tool that adds code MUST follow this structure\\n> exactly. It enforces high cohesion, low coupling, and zero cyclic dependencies by design.\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Core Architectural Rules (Read Before Writing Any Code)\\n\\n### Rule 1 — Dependency Direction is One-Way and Downward Only\\n```\\nshared/          ← no dependencies on any module (foundational)\\n    ↓\\n[module-A]       ← may depend on shared only\\n    ↓\\n[module-B]       ← may depend on shared + module-A\\n    ↓\\n[module-C]       ← may depend on shared + module-A + module-B\\n    ↓\\nadmin/           ← thin orchestration layer, depends on all modules but owns no domain\\n    ↓\\napp/             ← entry point only, no business logic\\n```\\n**A module NEVER imports from a module below it or at the same level.**\\n**If two modules need to talk, they do so through Spring Events — never direct service calls.**\\n\\n### Rule 2 — Three Ways Modules Communicate (Pick the Right One)\\n| Communication Type | When to Use | Mechanism |\\n|---|---|---|\\n| Query (sync, read-only) | Module B needs data that Module A owns | Module B calls Module A's **public API endpoint** (HTTP), never imports Module A's service class directly |\\n| Command (async, fire-and-forget) | Module A completed something and others should react | Module A publishes a **Spring ApplicationEvent**; interested modules register a listener |\\n| Shared contract | Enums, base classes, standard DTOs used by 2+ modules | Lives in `shared/common/` only — never duplicated |\\n\\n### Rule 3 — The ACL is the Only Door to External Systems\\nAny external API (Eventbrite, payment gateway, SMS provider, etc.) MUST be accessed\\nexclusively through the `shared/` ACL package. No module ever calls an external API directly.\\nIf a new external integration is needed, it goes in `shared/` first, then modules consume it\\nvia the ACL facade services.\\n\\n### Rule 4 — A Module Owns Its Own Database Tables\\nNo two modules share a JPA entity or database table. If Module B needs data from Module A's\\ntable, it calls Module A's API — it does NOT import Module A's `@Entity` class or `@Repository`.\\nViolating this rule creates hidden coupling at the database layer.\\n\\n### Rule 5 — The `admin/` Module Owns No Domain\\nThe admin module only orchestrates and aggregates. It delegates all writes to the owning module.\\nIt never defines its own `@Entity` or owns a database table. It is a read + orchestration layer.\\n\\nFor inter-module access, admin follows this rule:\\n- **Reads** — admin MAY import a module's `@Service` bean directly (same JVM, no overhead)\\n- **Writes** — admin MUST call the owning module's REST API (never bypass the module's own validation, events, and business rules)\\n\\n### Rule 6 — Mapping Logic Belongs in the Mapper Layer Only\\nConverting domain objects to API response DTOs and request DTOs to domain objects must be done\\nexclusively in the `mapper/` package. Controllers and services must never call `.builder()` on a\\nresponse DTO directly or manually map fields.\\n- **Controllers** — call service, return result. No mapping.\\n- **Services** — orchestrate domain + infra. No mapping.\\n- **Mappers** — the only place field translation happens. Use MapStruct.\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Project Layout\\n\\n```\\n[project-root]/\\n├── pom.xml                    ← Parent POM. All dependency versions managed here only.\\n├── shared/                    ← Internal library. Never imports from any module.\\n├── [module-name]/             ← One folder per bounded context.\\n├── admin/                     ← Thin orchestration layer. Owns no domain, no tables.\\n└── app/                       ← Spring Boot entry point. Zero business logic.\\n```\\n\\n---\\n\\n## shared/ Structure\\n\\nCross-cutting concerns only. Every module depends on this.\\n\\n```\\nshared/\\n└── src/main/java/com/[org]/shared/\\n    ├── [external-system-acl]/     ← One package per external system (e.g. razorpay/, eventbrite/)\\n    │   ├── client/                ← Raw HTTP calls only\\n    │   ├── config/                ← WebClient bean, rate limiter config\\n    │   ├── dto/request/           ← External system's raw request shapes\\n    │   ├── dto/response/          ← External system's raw response shapes\\n    │   ├── mapper/                ← Translates external DTOs → internal domain objects\\n    │   ├── service/               ← THE ONLY THING MODULES CALL. One service per capability.\\n    │   ├── webhook/               ← Receives inbound webhooks, dispatches as Spring Events\\n    │   └── resilience/            ← Circuit breaker + fallback config per external system\\n    │\\n    ├── security/\\n    │   ├── JwtTokenProvider       ← Generate + validate JWTs\\n    │   ├── JwtAuthenticationFilter← Spring Security filter, registered once\\n    │   └── SecurityConstants      ← Token TTLs, header names, role constants\\n    │\\n    ├── common/\\n    │   ├── domain/\\n    │   │   ├── BaseEntity         ← id, createdAt, updatedAt — all entities extend this\\n    │   │   └── Money              ← Value object for all monetary amounts. Never use primitives.\\n    │   ├── dto/\\n    │   │   ├── ApiResponse        ← Standard success response wrapper\\n    │   │   ├── PageResponse       ← Paginated list wrapper\\n    │   │   └── ErrorResponse      ← Standard error shape\\n    │   ├── enums/                 ← Enums shared across 2+ modules only\\n    │   └── exception/\\n    │       ├── GlobalExceptionHandler  ← @ControllerAdvice. One handler for the entire app.\\n    │       ├── ResourceNotFoundException   ← 404\\n    │       ├── BusinessRuleException       ← 422\\n    │       └── IntegrationException        ← 502\\n    │\\n    └── config/\\n        ├── RedisConfig            ← Shared Redis bean\\n        ├── ElasticsearchConfig    ← Shared ES client bean\\n        ├── CacheConfig            ← Cache names and TTLs\\n        ├── AsyncConfig            ← Thread pool for @Async\\n        ├── OpenApiConfig          ← Springdoc global config\\n        ├── WebConfig              ← Global CORS + request logging. Never use @CrossOrigin on controllers.\\n        └── TracingConfig          ← MDC traceId on every log line. Essential for prod debugging.\\n```\\n\\n---\\n\\n## [module-name]/ Structure\\n\\nOne folder per bounded context. Name reflects the domain, not the tech.\\n\\n```\\n[module-name]/\\n└── src/main/java/com/[org]/[module]/\\n    ├── api/\\n    │   ├── controller/            ← HTTP endpoints only. No business logic. Delegates to service.\\n    │   └── dto/\\n    │       ├── request/           ← [Action][Resource]Request\\n    │       └── response/          ← [Resource]Response. Flat, serializable, no JPA proxies.\\n    │\\n    ├── domain/                    ← Pure business logic. No Spring, no JPA, no infrastructure.\\n    │                                Entities have behavior (confirm(), cancel()) not just getters.\\n    │\\n    ├── service/                   ← Orchestrates domain + repositories + ACL + events. @Service beans.\\n    │                                One service per capability group. Methods = use cases not CRUD.\\n    │\\n    ├── repository/                ← @Repository interfaces only. No other module imports these.\\n    │\\n    ├── mapper/                    ← Domain ↔ DTO translation only. Use MapStruct.\\n    │\\n    ├── event/\\n    │   ├── published/             ← Events this module fires. Past tense. Primitives/IDs only.\\n    │   └── listener/              ← Events this module reacts to. No logic — delegate to service.\\n    │\\n    ├── statemachine/              ← OPTIONAL. Only if 4+ states with conditional transition guards.\\n    │   ├── [Resource]StateMachineConfig\\n    │   ├── [Resource]State\\n    │   ├── [Resource]Event\\n    │   ├── [Resource]StateMachineService\\n    │   ├── guard/\\n    │   └── action/\\n    │\\n    └── exception/                 ← OPTIONAL. Module-specific exceptions only.\\n```\\n\\n---\\n\\n## admin/ and app/\\n\\n```\\nadmin/\\n└── src/main/java/com/[org]/admin/\\n    ├── api/controller/            ← Admin-only endpoints (@PreAuthorize ROLE_ADMIN)\\n    └── service/                   ← Aggregates reads across modules. Delegates writes to module APIs.\\n\\napp/\\n└── src/main/java/com/[org]/\\n    ├── [ProjectName]Application   ← @SpringBootApplication. One class, nothing else.\\n    └── resources/\\n        ├── application.yml\\n        ├── application-local.yml\\n        ├── application-prod.yml\\n        └── db/migration/          ← Flyway migrations. Global version sequence. Never modify existing files.\\n```\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Anti-Patterns — Never Do These\\n\\n| Anti-Pattern | Why It's Wrong | Correct Approach |\\n|---|---|---|\\n| Module A imports `@Service` from Module B | Direct coupling — changes in B break A at compile time | Module A calls Module B's REST endpoint or listens to its Spring Event |\\n| Module A imports `@Entity` or `@Repository` from Module B | Hidden DB coupling — schema changes in B break A silently | Module A calls Module B's API to get the data it needs |\\n| Putting Eventbrite/Razorpay HTTP calls inside a module's service | Scattered integration — if the API changes, you hunt across modules | All external calls go through `shared/[system-acl]/service/` facades only |\\n| Sharing a database table between two modules | Breaks module autonomy — both modules must coordinate schema changes | Each module owns its own tables; sync via events or API calls |\\n| Putting business logic in a `@RestController` | Untestable and leaks domain logic into the HTTP layer | Controller delegates immediately to service; service owns all logic |\\n| Creating an `[Entity]Service` that just wraps CRUD | Anemic domain model — no real encapsulation | Put behavior on the entity itself; service orchestrates multi-step use cases |\\n| Publishing a Spring Event that contains a JPA `@Entity` | Entity may be a lazy-loaded proxy; causes serialization failures | Events carry only primitive values, IDs, or immutable value objects |\\n| Adding a new enum to `shared/common/enums/` for a single-module concept | Pollutes shared space with module-specific concerns | Single-module enums stay inside that module's `domain/` package |\\n| Writing business logic in `admin/` | Admin owns no domain; it becomes a second copy of truth | Admin delegates writes to the owning module's REST API; only reads and aggregates |\\n| Admin calling a module's `@Service` for writes | Bypasses the module's own validation, events, and business rules | Admin must call the owning module's REST API for all write operations |\\n| Mapping fields inside a `@Service` or `@RestController` | Pollutes orchestration/HTTP layers with translation logic; hard to test | All domain ↔ DTO mapping goes in `mapper/[Resource]Mapper` only |\\n| Using `@CrossOrigin` on individual controllers | Scattered CORS config — inconsistent across modules | Define CORS once in `shared/config/WebConfig` |\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Event Naming Conventions\\n\\n```\\nPublished events  →  Past tense + \\\"Event\\\" suffix\\n                     [WhatHappened]Event\\n                     e.g. BookingConfirmedEvent\\n                          SeatReleasedEvent\\n                          UserRegisteredEvent\\n                          PaymentFailedEvent\\n\\nListeners         →  [TriggerEvent]Listener\\n                     e.g. PaymentFailedListener (inside booking-inventory module)\\n                          BookingConfirmedListener (inside engagement module)\\n                          CartAssembledListener (inside payments module)\\n\\nEvent payload     →  Only primitives, IDs, enums, and value objects.\\n                     NEVER include @Entity objects in events.\\n                     NEVER include mutable collections.\\n```\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Module pom.xml Dependency Rules\\n\\n```xml\\n<!-- Every module pom.xml follows this exact pattern -->\\n<dependencies>\\n\\n  <!-- 1. Always: shared internal library -->\\n  <dependency>\\n    <groupId>com.[org]</groupId>\\n    <artifactId>shared</artifactId>\\n  </dependency>\\n\\n  <!-- 2. Only upstream modules (modules this one reads from) -->\\n  <!-- Example: booking-inventory reads from discovery-catalog -->\\n  <dependency>\\n    <groupId>com.[org]</groupId>\\n    <artifactId>[upstream-module]</artifactId>\\n  </dependency>\\n\\n  <!-- 3. NEVER depend on a peer or downstream module -->\\n  <!-- NEVER depend on admin/ -->\\n  <!-- NEVER depend on app/ -->\\n\\n</dependencies>\\n```\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Flyway Migration Naming\\n\\n```\\nV[auto-increment]__[verb]_[table_name].sql\\n\\nExamples:\\n  V1__create_users.sql\\n  V2__create_events.sql\\n  V7__add_wallet_balance_to_users.sql\\n  V12__create_seat_lock_audit_log.sql\\n\\nRules:\\n  - NEVER modify an existing migration file (Flyway will reject it in prod)\\n  - ALWAYS add a new file for any schema change\\n  - Version numbers are global across ALL modules — maintain a single sequence in app/\\n  - Table names use snake_case\\n  - Description uses snake_case with underscores\\n```\\n\"\nPOQ/Fake_Service/Fake_Seervice_TestSessions.md:1394:    \"output\": \"Chunk ID: 7b44dd\\nWall time: 0.0517 seconds\\nProcess exited with code 0\\nOriginal token count: 3366\\nOutput:\\n# Modular Monolith — Canonical Folder Structure Template\\n\\n> **Purpose:** This document is the canonical reference for building any new feature or module\\n> in this codebase. Every agent, developer, or tool that adds code MUST follow this structure\\n> exactly. It enforces high cohesion, low coupling, and zero cyclic dependencies by design.\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Core Architectural Rules (Read Before Writing Any Code)\\n\\n### Rule 1 — Dependency Direction is One-Way and Downward Only\\n```\\nshared/          ← no dependencies on any module (foundational)\\n    ↓\\n[module-A]       ← may depend on shared only\\n    ↓\\n[module-B]       ← may depend on shared + module-A\\n    ↓\\n[module-C]       ← may depend on shared + module-A + module-B\\n    ↓\\nadmin/           ← thin orchestration layer, depends on all modules but owns no domain\\n    ↓\\napp/             ← entry point only, no business logic\\n```\\n**A module NEVER imports from a module below it or at the same level.**\\n**If two modules need to talk, they do so through Spring Events — never direct service calls.**\\n\\n### Rule 2 — Three Ways Modules Communicate (Pick the Right One)\\n| Communication Type | When to Use | Mechanism |\\n|---|---|---|\\n| Query (sync, read-only) | Module B needs data that Module A owns | Module B calls Module A's **public API endpoint** (HTTP), never imports Module A's service class directly |\\n| Command (async, fire-and-forget) | Module A completed something and others should react | Module A publishes a **Spring ApplicationEvent**; interested modules register a listener |\\n| Shared contract | Enums, base classes, standard DTOs used by 2+ modules | Lives in `shared/common/` only — never duplicated |\\n\\n### Rule 3 — The ACL is the Only Door to External Systems\\nAny external API (Eventbrite, payment gateway, SMS provider, etc.) MUST be accessed\\nexclusively through the `shared/` ACL package. No module ever calls an external API directly.\\nIf a new external integration is needed, it goes in `shared/` first, then modules consume it\\nvia the ACL facade services.\\n\\n### Rule 4 — A Module Owns Its Own Database Tables\\nNo two modules share a JPA entity or database table. If Module B needs data from Module A's\\ntable, it calls Module A's API — it does NOT import Module A's `@Entity` class or `@Repository`.\\nViolating this rule creates hidden coupling at the database layer.\\n\\n### Rule 5 — The `admin/` Module Owns No Domain\\nThe admin module only orchestrates and aggregates. It delegates all writes to the owning module.\\nIt never defines its own `@Entity` or owns a database table. It is a read + orchestration layer.\\n\\nFor inter-module access, admin follows this rule:\\n- **Reads** — admin MAY import a module's `@Service` bean directly (same JVM, no overhead)\\n- **Writes** — admin MUST call the owning module's REST API (never bypass the module's own validation, events, and business rules)\\n\\n### Rule 6 — Mapping Logic Belongs in the Mapper Layer Only\\nConverting domain objects to API response DTOs and request DTOs to domain objects must be done\\nexclusively in the `mapper/` package. Controllers and services must never call `.builder()` on a\\nresponse DTO directly or manually map fields.\\n- **Controllers** — call service, return result. No mapping.\\n- **Services** — orchestrate domain + infra. No mapping.\\n- **Mappers** — the only place field translation happens. Use MapStruct.\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Project Layout\\n\\n```\\n[project-root]/\\n├── pom.xml                    ← Parent POM. All dependency versions managed here only.\\n├── shared/                    ← Internal library. Never imports from any module.\\n├── [module-name]/             ← One folder per bounded context.\\n├── admin/                     ← Thin orchestration layer. Owns no domain, no tables.\\n└── app/                       ← Spring Boot entry point. Zero business logic.\\n```\\n\\n---\\n\\n## shared/ Structure\\n\\nCross-cutting concerns only. Every module depen…96745 tokens truncated…turn result. No mapping.\\n- **Services** — orchestrate domain + infra. No mapping.\\n- **Mappers** — the only place field translation happens. Use MapStruct.\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Project Layout\\n\\n```\\n[project-root]/\\n├── pom.xml                    ← Parent POM. All dependency versions managed here only.\\n├── shared/                    ← Internal library. Never imports from any module.\\n├── [module-name]/             ← One folder per bounded context.\\n├── admin/                     ← Thin orchestration layer. Owns no domain, no tables.\\n└── app/                       ← Spring Boot entry point. Zero business logic.\\n```\\n\\n---\\n\\n## shared/ Structure\\n\\nCross-cutting concerns only. Every module depends on this.\\n\\n```\\nshared/\\n└── src/main/java/com/[org]/shared/\\n    ├── [external-system-acl]/     ← One package per external system (e.g. razorpay/, eventbrite/)\\n    │   ├── client/                ← Raw HTTP calls only\\n    │   ├── config/                ← WebClient bean, rate limiter config\\n    │   ├── dto/request/           ← External system's raw request shapes\\n    │   ├── dto/response/          ← External system's raw response shapes\\n    │   ├── mapper/                ← Translates external DTOs → internal domain objects\\n    │   ├── service/               ← THE ONLY THING MODULES CALL. One service per capability.\\n    │   ├── webhook/               ← Receives inbound webhooks, dispatches as Spring Events\\n    │   └── resilience/            ← Circuit breaker + fallback config per external system\\n    │\\n    ├── security/\\n    │   ├── JwtTokenProvider       ← Generate + validate JWTs\\n    │   ├── JwtAuthenticationFilter← Spring Security filter, registered once\\n    │   └── SecurityConstants      ← Token TTLs, header names, role constants\\n    │\\n    ├── common/\\n    │   ├── domain/\\n    │   │   ├── BaseEntity         ← id, createdAt, updatedAt — all entities extend this\\n    │   │   └── Money              ← Value object for all monetary amounts. Never use primitives.\\n    │   ├── dto/\\n    │   │   ├── ApiResponse        ← Standard success response wrapper\\n    │   │   ├── PageResponse       ← Paginated list wrapper\\n    │   │   └── ErrorResponse      ← Standard error shape\\n    │   ├── enums/                 ← Enums shared across 2+ modules only\\n    │   └── exception/\\n    │       ├── GlobalExceptionHandler  ← @ControllerAdvice. One handler for the entire app.\\n    │       ├── ResourceNotFoundException   ← 404\\n    │       ├── BusinessRuleException       ← 422\\n    │       └── IntegrationException        ← 502\\n    │\\n    └── config/\\n        ├── RedisConfig            ← Shared Redis bean\\n        ├── ElasticsearchConfig    ← Shared ES client bean\\n        ├── CacheConfig            ← Cache names and TTLs\\n        ├── AsyncConfig            ← Thread pool for @Async\\n        ├── OpenApiConfig          ← Springdoc global config\\n        ├── WebConfig              ← Global CORS + request logging. Never use @CrossOrigin on controllers.\\n        └── TracingConfig          ← MDC traceId on every log line. Essential for prod debugging.\\n```\\n\\n---\\n\\n## [module-name]/ Structure\\n\\nOne folder per bounded context. Flat, serializable, no JPA proxies.\\n    │\\n    ├── domain/                    ← Pure business logic. Delegates writes to module APIs.\\n\\napp/\\n└── src/main/java/com/[org]/\\n    ├── [ProjectName]Application   ← @SpringBootApplication. Global version sequence. Never modify existing files.\\n```\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Anti-Patterns — Never Do These\\n\\n| Anti-Pattern | Why It's Wrong | Correct Approach |\\n|---|---|---|\\n| Module A imports `@Service` from Module B | Direct coupling — changes in B break A at compile time | Module A calls Module B's REST endpoint or listens to its Spring Event |\\n| Module A imports `@Entity` or `@Repository` from Module B | Hidden DB coupling — schema changes in B break A silently | Module A calls Module B's API to get the data it needs |\\n| Putting Eventbrite/Razorpay HTTP calls inside a module's service | Scattered integration — if the API changes, you hunt across modules | All external calls go through `shared/[system-acl]/service/` facades only |\\n| Sharing a database table between two modules | Breaks module autonomy — both modules must coordinate schema changes | Each module owns its own tables; Global version sequence. Never modify existing files.\\n```\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Anti-Patterns — Never Do These\\n\\n| Anti-Pattern | Why It's Wrong | Correct Approach |\\n|---|---|---|\\n| Module A imports `@Service` from Module B | Direct coupling — changes in B break A at compile time | Module A calls Module B's REST endpoint or listens to its Spring Event |\\n| Module A imports `@Entity` or `@Repository` from Module B | Hidden DB coupling — schema changes in B break A silently | Module A calls Module B's API to get the data it needs |\\n| Putting Eventbrite/Razorpay HTTP calls inside a module's service | Scattered integration — if the API changes, you hunt across modules | All external calls go through `shared/[system-acl]/service/` facades only |\\n| Sharing a database table between two modules | Breaks module autonomy — both modules must coordinate schema changes | Each module owns its own tables; sync via events or API calls |\\n| Putting business logic in a `@RestController` | Untestable and leaks domain logic into the HTTP layer | Controller delegates immediately to service; service owns all logic |\\n| Creating an `[Entity]Service` that just wraps CRUD | Anemic domain model — no real encapsulation | Put behavior on the entity itself; service orchestrates multi-step use cases |\\n| Publishing a Spring Event that contains a JPA `@Entity` | Entity may be a lazy-loaded proxy; causes serialization failures | Events carry only primitive values, IDs, or immutable value objects |\\n| Adding a new enum to `shared/common/enums/` for a single-module concept | Pollutes shared space with module-specific concerns | Single-module enums stay inside that module's `domain/` package |\\n| Writing business logic in `admin/` | Admin owns no domain; it becomes a second copy of truth | Admin delegates writes to the owning module's REST API; only reads and aggregates |\\n| Admin calling a module's `@Service` for writes | Bypasses the module's own validation, events, and business rules | Admin must call the owning module's REST API for all write operations |\\n| Mapping fields inside a `@Service` or `@RestController` | Pollutes orchestration/HTTP layers with translation logic; hard to test | All domain ↔ DTO mapping goes in `mapper/[Resource]Mapper` only |\\n\"\n"
  }
}

Nothing else.\nPOQ/Fake_Service/Fake_Seervice_TestSessions.md:1382:    \"output\": \"Chunk ID: 7b44dd\\nWall time: 0.0517 seconds\\nProcess exited with code 0\\nOriginal token count: 3366\\nOutput:\\n# Modular Monolith — Canonical Folder Structure Template\\n\\n> **Purpose:** This document is the canonical reference for building any new feature or module\\n> in this codebase. Every agent, developer, or tool that adds code MUST follow this structure\\n> exactly. It enforces high cohesion, low coupling, and zero cyclic dependencies by design.\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Core Architectural Rules (Read Before Writing Any Code)\\n\\n### Rule 1 — Dependency Direction is One-Way and Downward Only\\n```\\nshared/          ← no dependencies on any module (foundational)\\n    ↓\\n[module-A]       ← may depend on shared only\\n    ↓\\n[module-B]       ← may depend on shared + module-A\\n    ↓\\n[module-C]       ← may depend on shared + module-A + module-B\\n    ↓\\nadmin/           ← thin orchestration layer, depends on all modules but owns no domain\\n    ↓\\napp/             ← entry point only, no business logic\\n```\\n**A module NEVER imports from a module below it or at the same level.**\\n**If two modules need to talk, they do so through Spring Events — never direct service calls.**\\n\\n### Rule 2 — Three Ways Modules Communicate (Pick the Right One)\\n| Communication Type | When to Use | Mechanism |\\n|---|---|---|\\n| Query (sync, read-only) | Module B needs data that Module A owns | Module B calls Module A's **public API endpoint** (HTTP), never imports Module A's service class directly |\\n| Command (async, fire-and-forget) | Module A completed something and others should react | Module A publishes a **Spring ApplicationEvent**; interested modules register a listener |\\n| Shared contract | Enums, base classes, standard DTOs used by 2+ modules | Lives in `shared/common/` only — never duplicated |\\n\\n### Rule 3 — The ACL is the Only Door to External Systems\\nAny external API (Eventbrite, payment gateway, SMS provider, etc.) MUST be accessed\\nexclusively through the `shared/` ACL package. No module ever calls an external API directly.\\nIf a new external integration is needed, it goes in `shared/` first, then modules consume it\\nvia the ACL facade services.\\n\\n### Rule 4 — A Module Owns Its Own Database Tables\\nNo two modules share a JPA entity or database table. If Module B needs data from Module A's\\ntable, it calls Module A's API — it does NOT import Module A's `@Entity` class or `@Repository`.\\nViolating this rule creates hidden coupling at the database layer.\\n\\n### Rule 5 — The `admin/` Module Owns No Domain\\nThe admin module only orchestrates and aggregates. It delegates all writes to the owning module.\\nIt never defines its own `@Entity` or owns a database table. It is a read + orchestration layer.\\n\\nFor inter-module access, admin follows this rule:\\n- **Reads** — admin MAY import a module's `@Service` bean directly (same JVM, no overhead)\\n- **Writes** — admin MUST call the owning module's REST API (never bypass the module's own validation, events, and business rules)\\n\\n### Rule 6 — Mapping Logic Belongs in the Mapper Layer Only\\nConverting domain objects to API response DTOs and request DTOs to domain objects must be done\\nexclusively in the `mapper/` package. Controllers and services must never call `.builder()` on a\\nresponse DTO directly or manually map fields.\\n- **Controllers** — call service, return result. No mapping.\\n- **Services** — orchestrate domain + infra. No mapping.\\n- **Mappers** — the only place field translation happens. Use MapStruct.\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Project Layout\\n\\n```\\n[project-root]/\\n├── pom.xml                    ← Parent POM. All dependency versions managed here only.\\n├── shared/                    ← Internal library. Never imports from any module.\\n├── [module-name]/             ← One folder per bounded context.\\n├── admin/                     ← Thin orchestration layer. Owns no domain, no tables.\\n└── app/                       ← Spring Boot entry point. Zero business logic.\\n```\\n\\n---\\n\\n## shared/ Structure\\n\\nCross-cutting concerns only. Every module depends on this.\\n\\n```\\nshared/\\n└── src/main/java/com/[org]/shared/\\n    ├── [external-system-acl]/     ← One package per external system (e.g. razorpay/, eventbrite/)\\n    │   ├── client/                ← Raw HTTP calls only\\n    │   ├── config/                ← WebClient bean, rate limiter config\\n    │   ├── dto/request/           ← External system's raw request shapes\\n    │   ├── dto/response/          ← External system's raw response shapes\\n    │   ├── mapper/                ← Translates external DTOs → internal domain objects\\n    │   ├── service/               ← THE ONLY THING MODULES CALL. One service per capability.\\n    │   ├── webhook/               ← Receives inbound webhooks, dispatches as Spring Events\\n    │   └── resilience/            ← Circuit breaker + fallback config per external system\\n    │\\n    ├── security/\\n    │   ├── JwtTokenProvider       ← Generate + validate JWTs\\n    │   ├── JwtAuthenticationFilter← Spring Security filter, registered once\\n    │   └── SecurityConstants      ← Token TTLs, header names, role constants\\n    │\\n    ├── common/\\n    │   ├── domain/\\n    │   │   ├── BaseEntity         ← id, createdAt, updatedAt — all entities extend this\\n    │   │   └── Money              ← Value object for all monetary amounts. Never use primitives.\\n    │   ├── dto/\\n    │   │   ├── ApiResponse        ← Standard success response wrapper\\n    │   │   ├── PageResponse       ← Paginated list wrapper\\n    │   │   └── ErrorResponse      ← Standard error shape\\n    │   ├── enums/                 ← Enums shared across 2+ modules only\\n    │   └── exception/\\n    │       ├── GlobalExceptionHandler  ← @ControllerAdvice. One handler for the entire app.\\n    │       ├── ResourceNotFoundException   ← 404\\n    │       ├── BusinessRuleException       ← 422\\n    │       └── IntegrationException        ← 502\\n    │\\n    └── config/\\n        ├── RedisConfig            ← Shared Redis bean\\n        ├── ElasticsearchConfig    ← Shared ES client bean\\n        ├── CacheConfig            ← Cache names and TTLs\\n        ├── AsyncConfig            ← Thread pool for @Async\\n        ├── OpenApiConfig          ← Springdoc global config\\n        ├── WebConfig              ← Global CORS + request logging. Never use @CrossOrigin on controllers.\\n        └── TracingConfig          ← MDC traceId on every log line. Essential for prod debugging.\\n```\\n\\n---\\n\\n## [module-name]/ Structure\\n\\nOne folder per bounded context. Name reflects the domain, not the tech.\\n\\n```\\n[module-name]/\\n└── src/main/java/com/[org]/[module]/\\n    ├── api/\\n    │   ├── controller/            ← HTTP endpoints only. No business logic. Delegates to service.\\n    │   └── dto/\\n    │       ├── request/           ← [Action][Resource]Request\\n    │       └── response/          ← [Resource]Response. Flat, serializable, no JPA proxies.\\n    │\\n    ├── domain/                    ← Pure business logic. No Spring, no JPA, no infrastructure.\\n    │                                Entities have behavior (confirm(), cancel()) not just getters.\\n    │\\n    ├── service/                   ← Orchestrates domain + repositories + ACL + events. @Service beans.\\n    │                                One service per capability group. Methods = use cases not CRUD.\\n    │\\n    ├── repository/                ← @Repository interfaces only. No other module imports these.\\n    │\\n    ├── mapper/                    ← Domain ↔ DTO translation only. Use MapStruct.\\n    │\\n    ├── event/\\n    │   ├── published/             ← Events this module fires. Past tense. Primitives/IDs only.\\n    │   └── listener/              ← Events this module reacts to. No logic — delegate to service.\\n    │\\n    ├── statemachine/              ← OPTIONAL. Only if 4+ states with conditional transition guards.\\n    │   ├── [Resource]StateMachineConfig\\n    │   ├── [Resource]State\\n    │   ├── [Resource]Event\\n    │   ├── [Resource]StateMachineService\\n    │   ├── guard/\\n    │   └── action/\\n    │\\n    └── exception/                 ← OPTIONAL. Module-specific exceptions only.\\n```\\n\\n---\\n\\n## admin/ and app/\\n\\n```\\nadmin/\\n└── src/main/java/com/[org]/admin/\\n    ├── api/controller/            ← Admin-only endpoints (@PreAuthorize ROLE_ADMIN)\\n    └── service/                   ← Aggregates reads across modules. Delegates writes to module APIs.\\n\\napp/\\n└── src/main/java/com/[org]/\\n    ├── [ProjectName]Application   ← @SpringBootApplication. One class, nothing else.\\n    └── resources/\\n        ├── application.yml\\n        ├── application-local.yml\\n        ├── application-prod.yml\\n        └── db/migration/          ← Flyway migrations. Global version sequence. Never modify existing files.\\n```\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Anti-Patterns — Never Do These\\n\\n| Anti-Pattern | Why It's Wrong | Correct Approach |\\n|---|---|---|\\n| Module A imports `@Service` from Module B | Direct coupling — changes in B break A at compile time | Module A calls Module B's REST endpoint or listens to its Spring Event |\\n| Module A imports `@Entity` or `@Repository` from Module B | Hidden DB coupling — schema changes in B break A silently | Module A calls Module B's API to get the data it needs |\\n| Putting Eventbrite/Razorpay HTTP calls inside a module's service | Scattered integration — if the API changes, you hunt across modules | All external calls go through `shared/[system-acl]/service/` facades only |\\n| Sharing a database table between two modules | Breaks module autonomy — both modules must coordinate schema changes | Each module owns its own tables; sync via events or API calls |\\n| Putting business logic in a `@RestController` | Untestable and leaks domain logic into the HTTP layer | Controller delegates immediately to service; service owns all logic |\\n| Creating an `[Entity]Service` that just wraps CRUD | Anemic domain model — no real encapsulation | Put behavior on the entity itself; service orchestrates multi-step use cases |\\n| Publishing a Spring Event that contains a JPA `@Entity` | Entity may be a lazy-loaded proxy; causes serialization failures | Events carry only primitive values, IDs, or immutable value objects |\\n| Adding a new enum to `shared/common/enums/` for a single-module concept | Pollutes shared space with module-specific concerns | Single-module enums stay inside that module's `domain/` package |\\n| Writing business logic in `admin/` | Admin owns no domain; it becomes a second copy of truth | Admin delegates writes to the owning module's REST API; only reads and aggregates |\\n| Admin calling a module's `@Service` for writes | Bypasses the module's own validation, events, and business rules | Admin must call the owning module's REST API for all write operations |\\n| Mapping fields inside a `@Service` or `@RestController` | Pollutes orchestration/HTTP layers with translation logic; hard to test | All domain ↔ DTO mapping goes in `mapper/[Resource]Mapper` only |\\n| Using `@CrossOrigin` on individual controllers | Scattered CORS config — inconsistent across modules | Define CORS once in `shared/config/WebConfig` |\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Event Naming Conventions\\n\\n```\\nPublished events  →  Past tense + \\\"Event\\\" suffix\\n                     [WhatHappened]Event\\n                     e.g. BookingConfirmedEvent\\n                          SeatReleasedEvent\\n                          UserRegisteredEvent\\n                          PaymentFailedEvent\\n\\nListeners         →  [TriggerEvent]Listener\\n                     e.g. PaymentFailedListener (inside booking-inventory module)\\n                          BookingConfirmedListener (inside engagement module)\\n                          CartAssembledListener (inside payments module)\\n\\nEvent payload     →  Only primitives, IDs, enums, and value objects.\\n                     NEVER include @Entity objects in events.\\n                     NEVER include mutable collections.\\n```\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Module pom.xml Dependency Rules\\n\\n```xml\\n<!-- Every module pom.xml follows this exact pattern -->\\n<dependencies>\\n\\n  <!-- 1. Always: shared internal library -->\\n  <dependency>\\n    <groupId>com.[org]</groupId>\\n    <artifactId>shared</artifactId>\\n  </dependency>\\n\\n  <!-- 2. Only upstream modules (modules this one reads from) -->\\n  <!-- Example: booking-inventory reads from discovery-catalog -->\\n  <dependency>\\n    <groupId>com.[org]</groupId>\\n    <artifactId>[upstream-module]</artifactId>\\n  </dependency>\\n\\n  <!-- 3. NEVER depend on a peer or downstream module -->\\n  <!-- NEVER depend on admin/ -->\\n  <!-- NEVER depend on app/ -->\\n\\n</dependencies>\\n```\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Flyway Migration Naming\\n\\n```\\nV[auto-increment]__[verb]_[table_name].sql\\n\\nExamples:\\n  V1__create_users.sql\\n  V2__create_events.sql\\n  V7__add_wallet_balance_to_users.sql\\n  V12__create_seat_lock_audit_log.sql\\n\\nRules:\\n  - NEVER modify an existing migration file (Flyway will reject it in prod)\\n  - ALWAYS add a new file for any schema change\\n  - Version numbers are global across ALL modules — maintain a single sequence in app/\\n  - Table names use snake_case\\n  - Description uses snake_case with underscores\\n```\\n\"\nPOQ/Fake_Service/Fake_Seervice_TestSessions.md:1394:    \"output\": \"Chunk ID: 7b44dd\\nWall time: 0.0517 seconds\\nProcess exited with code 0\\nOriginal token count: 3366\\nOutput:\\n# Modular Monolith — Canonical Folder Structure Template\\n\\n> **Purpose:** This document is the canonical reference for building any new feature or module\\n> in this codebase. Global version sequence. Never modify existing files.\\n```\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Anti-Patterns — Never Do These\\n\\n| Anti-Pattern | Why It's Wrong | Correct Approach |\\n|---|---|---|\\n| Module A imports `@Service` from Module B | Direct coupling — changes in B break A at compile time | Module A calls Module B's REST endpoint or listens to its Spring Event |\\n| Module A imports `@Entity` or `@Repository` from Module B | Hidden DB coupling — schema changes in B break A silently | Module A calls Module B's API to get the data it needs |\\n| Putting Eventbrite/Razorpay HTTP calls inside a module's service | Scattered integration — if the API changes, you hunt across modules | All external calls go through `shared/[system-acl]/service/` facades only |\\n| Sharing a database table between two modules | Breaks module autonomy — both modules must coordinate schema changes | Each module owns its own tables; sync via events or API calls |\\n| Putting business logic in a `@RestController` | Untestable and leaks domain logic into the HTTP layer | Controller delegates immediately to service; service owns all logic |\\n| Creating an `[Entity]Service` that just wraps CRUD | Anemic domain model — no real encapsulation | Put behavior on the entity itself; service orchestrates multi-step use cases |\\n| Publishing a Spring Event that contains a JPA `@Entity` | Entity may be a lazy-loaded proxy; causes serialization failures | Events carry only primitive values, IDs, or immutable value objects |\\n| Adding a new enum to `shared/common/enums/` for a single-module concept | Pollutes shared space with module-specific concerns | Single-module enums stay inside that module's `domain/` package |\\n| Writing business logic in `admin/` | Admin owns no domain; it becomes a second copy of truth | Admin delegates writes to the owning module's REST API; only reads and aggregates |\\n| Admin calling a module's `@Service` for writes | Bypasses the module's own validation, events, and business rules | Admin must call the owning module's REST API for all write operations |\\n| Mapping fields inside a `@Service` or `@RestController` | Pollutes orchestration/HTTP layers with translation logic; hard to test | All domain ↔ DTO mapping goes in `mapper/[Resource]Mapper` only |\\n\"\nPOQ/Search-Catalog/Search_Catalog_SpecGenrating_Planning.md:2816:    \"output\": \"Chunk ID: 56501c\\nWall time: 0.0515 seconds\\nProcess exited with code 0\\nOriginal token count: 2836\\nOutput:\\n# Modular Monolith — Canonical Folder Structure Template\\n\\n> **Purpose:** This document is the canonical reference for building any new feature or module\\n> in this codebase. Every agent, developer, or tool that adds code MUST follow this structure\\n> exactly. It enforces high cohesion, low coupling, and zero cyclic dependencies by design.\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Core Architectural Rules (Read Before Writing Any Code)\\n\\n### Rule 1 — Dependency Direction is One-Way and Downward Only\\n```\\nshared/          ← no dependencies on any module (foundational)\\n    ↓\\n[module-A]       ← may depend on shared only\\n    ↓\\n[module-B]       ← may depend on shared + module-A\\n    ↓\\n[module-C]       ← may depend on shared + module-A + module-B\\n    ↓\\nadmin/           ← thin orchestration layer, depends on all modules but owns no domain\\n    ↓\\napp/             ← entry point only, no business logic\\n```\\n**A module NEVER imports from a module below it or at the same level.**\\n**If two modules need to talk, they do so through Spring Events — never direct service calls.**\\n\\n### Rule 2 — Three Ways Modules Communicate (Pick the Right One)\\n| Communication Type | When to Use | Mechanism |\\n|---|---|---|\\n| Query (sync, read-only) | Module B needs data that Module A owns | Module B calls Module A's **public API endpoint** (HTTP), never imports Module A's service class directly |\\n| Command (async, fire-and-forget) | Module A completed something and others should react | Module A publishes a **Spring ApplicationEvent**; interested modules register a listener |\\n| Shared contract | Enums, base classes, standard DTOs used by 2+ modules | Lives in `shared/common/` only — never duplicated |\\n\\n### Rule 3 — The ACL is the Only Door to External Systems\\nAny external API (Eventbrite, payment gateway, SMS provider, etc.) MUST be accessed\\nexclusively through the `shared/` ACL package. No module ever calls an external API directly.\\nIf a new external integration is needed, it goes in `shared/` first, then modules consume it\\nvia the ACL facade services.\\n\\n### Rule 4 — A Module Owns Its Own Database Tables\\nNo two modules share a JPA entity or database table. If Module B needs data from Module A's\\ntable, it calls Module A's API — it does NOT import Module A's `@Entity` class or `@Repository`.\\nViolating this rule creates hidden coupling at the database layer.\\n\\n### Rule 5 — The `admin/` Module Owns No Domain\\nThe admin module only orchestrates and aggregates. It delegates all writes to the owning module.\\nIt never defines its own `@Entity` or owns a database table. It is a read + orchestration layer.\\n\\nFor inter-module access, admin follows this rule:\\n- **Reads** — admin MAY import a module's `@Service` bean directly (same JVM, no overhead)\\n- **Writes** — admin MUST call the owning module's REST API (never bypass the module's own validation, events, and business rules)\\n\\n### Rule 6 — Mapping Logic Belongs in the Mapper Layer Only\\nConverting domain objects to API response DTOs and request DTOs to domain objects must be done\\nexclusively in the `mapper/` package. Controllers and services must never call `.builder()` on a\\nresponse DTO directly or manually map fields.\\n- **Controllers** — call service, return result. No mapping.\\n- **Services** — orchestrate domain + infra. No mapping.\\n- **Mappers** — the only place field translation happens. Use MapStruct.\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Project Layout\\n\\n```\\n[project-root]/\\n├── pom.xml                    ← Parent POM. All dependency versions managed here only.\\n├── shared/                    ← Internal library. Never imports from any module.\\n├── [module-name]/             ← One folder per bounded context.\\n├── admin/                     ← Thin orchestration layer. Owns no domain, no tables.\\n└── app/                       ← Spring Boot entry point. Zero business logic.\\n```\\n\\n---\\n\\n## shared/ Structure\\n\\nCross-cutting concerns only. Every module depends on this.\\n\\n```\\nshared/\\n└── src/main/java/com/[org]/shared/\\n    ├── [external-system-acl]/     ← One package per external system (e.g. razorpay/, eventbrite/)\\n    │   ├── client/                ← Raw HTTP calls only\\n    │   ├── config/                ← WebClient bean, rate limiter config\\n    │   ├── dto/request/           ← External system's raw request shapes\\n    │   ├── dto/response/          ← External system's raw response shapes\\n    │   ├── mapper/                ← Translates external DTOs → internal domain objects\\n    │   ├── service/               ← THE ONLY THING MODULES CALL. One service per capability.\\n    │   ├── webhook/               ← Receives inbound webhooks, dispatches as Spring Events\\n    │   └── resilience/            ← Circuit breaker + fallback config per external system\\n    │\\n    ├── security/\\n    │   ├── JwtTokenProvider       ← Generate + validate JWTs\\n    │   ├── JwtAuthenticationFilter← Spring Security filter, registered once\\n    │   └── SecurityConstants      ← Token TTLs, header names, role constants\\n    │\\n    ├── common/\\n    │   ├── domain/\\n    │   │   ├── BaseEntity         ← id, createdAt, updatedAt — all entities extend this\\n    │   │   └── Money              ← Value object for all monetary amounts. Never use primitives.\\n    │   ├── dto/\\n    │   │   ├── ApiResponse        ← Standard success response wrapper\\n    │   │   ├── PageResponse       ← Paginated list wrapper\\n    │   │   └── ErrorResponse      ← Standard error shape\\n    │   ├── enums/                 ← Enums shared across 2+ modules only\\n    │   └── exception/\\n    │       ├── GlobalExceptionHandler  ← @ControllerAdvice. One handler for the entire app.\\n    │       ├── ResourceNotFoundException   ← 404\\n    │       ├── BusinessRuleException       ← 422\\n    │       └── IntegrationException        ← 502\\n    │\\n    └── config/\\n        ├── RedisConfig            ← Shared Redis bean\\n        ├── ElasticsearchConfig    ← Shared ES client bean\\n        ├── CacheConfig            ← Cache names and TTLs\\n        ├── AsyncConfig            ← Thread pool for @Async\\n        ├── OpenApiConfig          ← Springdoc global config\\n        ├── WebConfig              ← Global CORS + request logging. Never use @CrossOrigin on controllers.\\n        └── TracingConfig          ← MDC traceId on every log line. Essential for prod debugging.\\n```\\n\\n---\\n\\n## [module-name]/ Structure\\n\\nOne folder per bounded context. Name reflects the domain, not the tech.\\n\\n```\\n[module-name]/\\n└── src/main/java/com/[org]/[module]/\\n    ├── api/\\n    │   ├── controller/            ← HTTP endpoints only. No business logic. Delegates to service.\\n    │   └── dto/\\n    │       ├── request/           ← [Action][Resource]Request\\n    │       └── response/          ← [Resource]Response. Flat, serializable, no JPA proxies.\\n    │\\n    ├── domain/                    ← Pure business logic. No Spring, no JPA, no infrastructure.\\n    │                                Entities have behavior (confirm(), cancel()) not just getters.\\n    │\\n    ├── service/                   ← Orchestrates domain + repositories + ACL + events. @Service beans.\\n    │                                One service per capability group. Methods = use cases not CRUD.\\n    │\\n    ├── repository/                ← @Repository interfaces only. No other module imports these.\\n    │\\n    ├── mapper/                    ← Domain ↔ DTO translation only. Use MapStruct.\\n    │\\n    ├── event/\\n    │   ├── published/             ← Events this module fires. Past tense. Primitives/IDs only.\\n    │   └── listener/              ← Events this module reacts to. No logic — delegate to service.\\n    │\\n    ├── statemachine/              ← OPTIONAL. Only if 4+ states with conditional transition guards.\\n    │   ├── [Resource]StateMachineConfig\\n    │   ├── [Resource]State\\n    │   ├── [Resource]Event\\n    │   ├── [Resource]StateMachineService\\n    │   ├── guard/\\n    │   └── action/\\n    │\\n    └── exception/                 ← OPTIONAL. Module-specific exceptions only.\\n```\\n\\n---\\n\\n## admin/ and app/\\n\\n```\\nadmin/\\n└── src/main/java/com/[org]/admin/\\n    ├── api/controller/            ← Admin-only endpoints (@PreAuthorize ROLE_ADMIN)\\n    └── service/                   ← Aggregates reads across modules. Delegates writes to module APIs.\\n\\napp/\\n└── src/main/java/com/[org]/\\n    ├── [ProjectName]Application   ← @SpringBootApplication. One class, nothing else.\\n    └── resources/\\n        ├── application.yml\\n        ├── application-local.yml\\n        ├── application-prod.yml\\n        └── db/migration/          ← Flyway migrations. Global version sequence. Never modify existing files.\\n```\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Anti-Patterns — Never Do These\\n\\n| Anti-Pattern | Why It's Wrong | Correct Approach |\\n|---|---|---|\\n| Module A imports `@Service` from Module B | Direct coupling — changes in B break A at compile time | Module A calls Module B's REST endpoint or listens to its Spring Event |\\n| Module A imports `@Entity` or `@Repository` from Module B | Hidden DB coupling — schema changes in B break A silently | Module A calls Module B's API to get the data it needs |\\n| Putting Eventbrite/Razorpay HTTP calls inside a module's service | Scattered integration — if the API changes, you hunt across modules | All external calls go through `shared/[system-acl]/service/` facades only |\\n| Sharing a database table between two modules | Breaks module autonomy — both modules must coordinate schema changes | Each module owns its own tables; sync via events or API calls |\\n| Putting business logic in a `@RestController` | Untestable and leaks domain logic into the HTTP layer | Controller delegates immediately to service; service owns all logic |\\n| Creating an `[Entity]Service` that just wraps CRUD | Anemic domain model — no real encapsulation | Put behavior on the entity itself; service orchestrates multi-step use cases |\\n| Publishing a Spring Event that contains a JPA `@Entity` | Entity may be a lazy-loaded proxy; causes serialization failures | Events carry only primitive values, IDs, or immutable value objects |\\n| Adding a new enum to `shared/common/enums/` for a single-module concept | Pollutes shared space with module-specific concerns | Single-module enums stay inside that module's `domain/` package |\\n| Writing business logic in `admin/` | Admin owns no domain; it becomes a second copy of truth | Admin delegates writes to the owning module's REST API; only reads and aggregates |\\n| Admin calling a module's `@Service` for writes | Bypasses the module's own validation, events, and business rules | Admin must call the owning module's REST API for all write operations |\\n| Mapping fields inside a `@Service` or `@RestController` | Pollutes orchestration/HTTP layers with translation logic; hard to test | All domain ↔ DTO mapping goes in `mapper/[Resource]Mapper` only |\\n\"\n"
  }
}
```
