    "arguments": "{\"cmd\":\"cat > admin/src/main/java/com/eventplatform/admin/api/controller/OrgEventbriteController.java <<'EOF'\\npackage com.eventplatform.admin.api.controller;\\n\\nimport com.eventplatform.admin.service.OrgOAuthService;\\nimport com.eventplatform.shared.eventbrite.domain.OrganizationAuth;\\nimport com.eventplatform.shared.security.Roles;\\nimport jakarta.servlet.http.HttpServletResponse;\\nimport java.io.IOException;\\nimport java.time.Instant;\\nimport org.springframework.http.HttpStatus;\\nimport org.springframework.security.access.prepost.PreAuthorize;\\nimport org.springframework.web.bind.annotation.DeleteMapping;\\nimport org.springframework.web.bind.annotation.GetMapping;\\nimport org.springframework.web.bind.annotation.PathVariable;\\nimport org.springframework.web.bind.annotation.PostMapping;\\nimport org.springframework.web.bind.annotation.RequestMapping;\\nimport org.springframework.web.bind.annotation.RequestParam;\\nimport org.springframework.web.bind.annotation.ResponseStatus;\\nimport org.springframework.web.bind.annotation.RestController;\\n\\n@RestController\\n@RequestMapping(\\\"/api/v1/admin/orgs/{orgId}/eventbrite\\\")\\npublic class OrgEventbriteController {\\n\\n    private final OrgOAuthService orgOAuthService;\\n\\n    public OrgEventbriteController(OrgOAuthService orgOAuthService) {\\n        this.orgOAuthService = orgOAuthService;\\n    }\\n\\n    @PostMapping(\\\"/connect\\\")\\n    @PreAuthorize(\\\"hasRole('\\\" + Roles.ADMIN + \\\"')\\\")\\n    public AuthorizationResponse connect(@PathVariable(\\\"orgId\\\") Long orgId) {\\n        String url = orgOAuthService.buildAuthorizationUrl(orgId);\\n        return new AuthorizationResponse(url);\\n    }\\n\\n    @GetMapping(\\\"/callback\\\")\\n    @ResponseStatus(HttpStatus.FOUND)\\n    public void callback(\\n        @PathVariable(\\\"orgId\\\") Long orgId,\\n        @RequestParam(\\\"code\\\") String code,\\n        @RequestParam(\\\"state\\\") String state,\\n        HttpServletResponse response\\n    ) throws IOException {\\n        orgOAuthService.handleCallback(orgId, code, state);\\n        response.sendRedirect(\\\"/admin/dashboard\\\");\\n    }\\n\\n    @GetMapping(\\\"/status\\\")\\n    @PreAuthorize(\\\"hasRole('\\\" + Roles.ADMIN + \\\"')\\\")\\n    public OrgAuthStatusResponse status(@PathVariable(\\\"orgId\\\") Long orgId) {\\n        OrganizationAuth auth = orgOAuthService.getStatus(orgId);\\n        return new OrgAuthStatusResponse(\\n            auth.getOrganizationId(),\\n            auth.getEbOrganizationId(),\\n            auth.getStatus().name(),\\n            auth.getExpiresAt(),\\n            auth.getCreatedAt()\\n        );\\n    }\\n\\n    @DeleteMapping(\\\"/disconnect\\\")\\n    @ResponseStatus(HttpStatus.NO_CONTENT)\\n    @PreAuthorize(\\\"hasRole('\\\" + Roles.ADMIN + \\\"')\\\")\\n    public void disconnect(@PathVariable(\\\"orgId\\\") Long orgId) {\\n        orgOAuthService.disconnect(orgId);\\n    }\\n\\n    public record AuthorizationResponse(String authorizationUrl) {\\n    }\\n\\n    public record OrgAuthStatusResponse(\\n        Long orgId,\\n        String ebOrganizationId,\\n        String status,\\n        Instant expiresAt,\\n        Instant connectedAt\\n    ) {\\n    }\\n}\\nEOF\"}",
    "call_id": "call_C9bRKcuI4G8DkswWHiV43SW1"
  }
}

{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-03T10:22:48.796Z",
  "type": "response_item",
  "payload": {
    "type": "function_call",
    "name": "exec_command",
    "arguments": "{\"cmd\":\"cat > admin/src/main/java/com/eventplatform/admin/service/client/SchedulingAdminClient.java <<'EOF'\\npackage com.eventplatform.admin.service.client;\\n\\nimport com.eventplatform.shared.common.dto.PageResponse;\\nimport com.eventplatform.shared.common.exception.IntegrationException;\\nimport java.util.List;\\nimport org.springframework.beans.factory.annotation.Value;\\nimport org.springframework.core.ParameterizedTypeReference;\\nimport org.springframework.stereotype.Service;\\nimport org.springframework.web.client.RestClient;\\nimport org.springframework.web.client.RestClientResponseException;\\n\\n@Service\\npublic class SchedulingAdminClient {\\n\\n    private final RestClient restClient;\\n\\n    public SchedulingAdminClient(@Value(\\\"${app.internal-base-url:http://localhost:8080}\\\") String baseUrl) {\\n        this.restClient = RestClient.builder().baseUrl(baseUrl).build();\\n    }\\n\\n    public List<SchedulingSlotResponse> getPendingSyncSlots(Long orgId) {\\n        try {\\n            PageResponse<SchedulingSlotResponse> response = restClient.get()\\n                .uri(uriBuilder -> uriBuilder\\n                    .path(\\\"/api/v1/scheduling/slots\\\")\\n                    .queryParam(\\\"orgId\\\", orgId)\\n                    .queryParam(\\\"status\\\", \\\"PENDING_SYNC\\\")\\n                    .build())\\n                .retrieve()\\n                .body(new ParameterizedTypeReference<>() {});\\n            return response == null ? List.of() : response.items();\\n        } catch (RestClientResponseException ex) {\\n            throw new IntegrationException(\\\"Failed to fetch pending sync slots\\\", \\\"SCHEDULING_INTEGRATION_ERROR\\\");\\n        }\\n    }\\n\\n    public List<SchedulingSlotResponse> getMismatchSlots(Long orgId) {\\n        try {\\n            PageResponse<SchedulingSlotResponse> response = restClient.get()\\n                .uri(uriBuilder -> uriBuilder\\n                    .path(\\\"/api/v1/scheduling/slots/mismatches\\\")\\n                    .queryParam(\\\"orgId\\\", orgId)\\n                    .build())\\n                .retrieve()\\n                .body(new ParameterizedTypeReference<>() {});\\n            return response == null ? List.of() : response.items();\\n        } catch (RestClientResponseException ex) {\\n            throw new IntegrationException(\\\"Failed to fetch mismatch slots\\\", \\\"SCHEDULING_INTEGRATION_ERROR\\\");\\n        }\\n    }\\n}\\nEOF\"}",
    "call_id": "call_8lxJn7qvoDnoFIMLUv6kHueY"
  }
}

List.of() : response.venues();\\n        } catch (RestClientResponseException ex) {\\n            throw new IntegrationException(\\\"Failed to fetch flagged venues\\\", \\\"CATALOG_INTEGRATION_ERROR\\\");\\n        }\\n    }\\n}\\nEOF\"}",

## Entry 1137
**Timestamp:** 2026-03-03T10:23:17.892Z
**Role/Type:** response_item
**Content:**
```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-03T10:23:17.892Z",
  "type": "response_item",
  "payload": {
    "type": "function_call",
    "name": "exec_command",
    "arguments": "{\"cmd\":\"cat > admin/src/main/java/com/eventplatform/admin/service/OrgDashboardService.java <<'EOF'\\npackage com.eventplatform.admin.service;\\n\\nimport com.eventplatform.admin.service.client.AdminVenueResponse;\\nimport com.eventplatform.admin.service.client.CatalogAdminClient;\\nimport com.eventplatform.admin.service.client.SchedulingAdminClient;\\nimport com.eventplatform.admin.service.client.SchedulingSlotResponse;\\nimport java.util.List;\\nimport org.springframework.stereotype.Service;\\n\\n@Service\\npublic class OrgDashboardService {\\n\\n    private final SchedulingAdminClient schedulingAdminClient;\\n    private final CatalogAdminClient catalogAdminClient;\\n\\n    public OrgDashboardService(\\n        SchedulingAdminClient schedulingAdminClient,\\n        CatalogAdminClient catalogAdminClient\\n    ) {\\n        this.schedulingAdminClient = schedulingAdminClient;\\n        this.catalogAdminClient = catalogAdminClient;\\n    }\\n\\n    public SlotFlaggedResponse getFlaggedSlots(Long orgId) {\\n        List<SchedulingSlotResponse> pending = schedulingAdminClient.getPendingSyncSlots(orgId).stream()\\n            .filter(slot -> slot.syncAttemptCount() > 0)\\n            .toList();\\n        List<SchedulingSlotResponse> mismatches = schedulingAdminClient.getMismatchSlots(orgId);\\n        return new SlotFlaggedResponse(pending, mismatches);\\n    }\\n\\n    public List<AdminVenueResponse> getFlaggedVenues(Long orgId) {\\n        return catalogAdminClient.getFlaggedVenues(orgId);\\n    }\\n\\n    public record SlotFlaggedResponse(\\n        List<SchedulingSlotResponse> pendingSyncFailed,\\n        List<SchedulingSlotResponse> activeWithEbMismatch\\n    ) {\\n    }\\n}\\nEOF\"}",
    "call_id": "call_kOrUc6oJSfriB6FmqFEdQ1V2"
  }
}
```

## Entry 1143
**Timestamp:** 2026-03-03T10:23:41.041Z
**Role/Type:** response_item
**Content:**
```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-03T10:23:41.041Z",
  "type": "response_item",
  "payload": {
    "type": "function_call",
    "name": "exec_command",
    "arguments": "{\"cmd\":\"cat > admin/src/main/java/com/eventplatform/admin/api/controller/OrgDashboardController.java <<'EOF'\\npackage com.eventplatform.admin.api.controller;\\n\\nimport com.eventplatform.admin.service.OrgDashboardService;\\nimport com.eventplatform.admin.service.OrgDashboardService.SlotFlaggedResponse;\\nimport com.eventplatform.admin.service.client.AdminVenueResponse;\\nimport com.eventplatform.shared.security.Roles;\\nimport java.util.List;\\nimport org.springframework.security.access.prepost.PreAuthorize;\\nimport org.springframework.web.bind.annotation.GetMapping;\\nimport org.springframework.web.bind.annotation.RequestMapping;\\nimport org.springframework.web.bind.annotation.RequestParam;\\nimport org.springframework.web.bind.annotation.RestController;\\n\\n@RestController\\n@RequestMapping(\\\"/api/v1/admin\\\")\\npublic class OrgDashboardController {\\n\\n    private final OrgDashboardService dashboardService;\\n\\n    public OrgDashboardController(OrgDashboardService dashboardService) {\\n        this.dashboardService = dashboardService;\\n    }\\n\\n    @GetMapping(\\\"/scheduling/slots/flagged\\\")\\n    @PreAuthorize(\\\"hasRole('\\\" + Roles.ADMIN + \\\"')\\\")\\n    public SlotFlaggedResponse flaggedSlots(@RequestParam(\\\"orgId\\\") Long orgId) {\\n        return dashboardService.getFlaggedSlots(orgId);\\n    }\\n\\n    @GetMapping(\\\"/dashboard/venues/flagged\\\")\\n    @PreAuthorize(\\\"hasRole('\\\" + Roles.ADMIN + \\\"')\\\")\\n    public List<AdminVenueResponse> flaggedVenues(@RequestParam(\\\"orgId\\\") Long orgId) {\\n        return dashboardService.getFlaggedVenues(orgId);\\n    }\\n}\\nEOF\"}",
    "call_id": "call_FEOnGdTdOUBr33hjUwJyxkE2"
  }
}
```

    "input": "*** Begin Patch\n*** Update File: app/src/main/resources/application.yaml\n@@\n app:\n   default-org-id: 1\n+  internal-base-url: http://localhost:8080\n \n eventbrite:\n+  oauth:\n+    client-id: local-client-id\n+    redirect-uri: http://localhost:8080/api/v1/admin/orgs/1/eventbrite/callback\n+    encryption-key: MDEyMzQ1Njc4OWFiY2RlZjAxMjM0NTY3ODlhYmNkZWY=\n+  retry:\n+    max-attempts: 3\n+    initial-backoff-ms: 250\n+    max-backoff-ms: 5000\n   webhook:\n     secret: local-dev-secret\n     endpoint-url: http://localhost:8080/admin/v1/webhooks/eventbrite\n+\n+scheduling:\n+  turnaround-gap-minutes: 60\n*** End Patch"
  }
}

    "status": "completed",
    "call_id": "call_uaaowcJNtSdCC0AAGDlFwyUB",
    "name": "apply_patch",
    "input": "*** Begin Patch\n*** Update File: shared/src/main/java/com/eventplatform/shared/security/SecurityConfig.java\n@@\n             .authorizeHttpRequests(auth -> auth\n-                .requestMatchers(\"/api/v1/catalog/**\", \"/admin/v1/webhooks/eventbrite\").permitAll()\n+                .requestMatchers(\n+                    \"/api/v1/catalog/**\",\n+                    \"/api/v1/admin/orgs/*/eventbrite/callback\",\n+                    \"/admin/v1/webhooks/eventbrite\"\n+                ).permitAll()\n                 .anyRequest().authenticated()\n             )\n*** End Patch"
  }
}

{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-03T10:25:05.506Z",
  "type": "event_msg",
  "payload": {
    "type": "agent_reasoning",
    "text": "**Integrating scheduling and booking-inventory updates**\n\nI'm aligning scheduling with event listeners, attaching pricing tiers properly, enhancing error detail propagation, and refining sync failure tracking in ShowSlotService and SlotReconciliationJob. Also, I'm integrating booking-inventory calls to scheduling's authenticated endpoints while managing shared event handling carefully."
  }
}
```

## Entry 1172
**Timestamp:** 2026-03-03T10:25:05.511Z
**Role/Type:** event_msg
**Content:**
```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-03T10:25:05.511Z",
  "type": "event_msg",
  "payload": {
    "type": "agent_reasoning",
    "text": "**Completing multi-module integration and cleanup**\n\nI've updated scheduling and discovery-catalog with new domain mappings, event listeners, and repository queries, ensured consistent event usage via shared, refined exceptions to include details, and aligned authentication handling on internal clients. Packaging and build updates include adding scheduling, booking-inventory, and admin modules with proper dependencies and migrated schema versions."
  }
}
```

  }
}

{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-03T10:25:24.141Z",
  "type": "event_msg",
  "payload": {
    "type": "agent_reasoning",
    "text": "**Reviewing module updates and mappings**\n\nThe admin and booking-inventory modules are added to the root, and the scheduling module's enums folder is noted as empty. The app packaging may need updates for new modules. In `VenueService`, potential null handling issues with address formatting and sync status updates on EB failures are identified. The scheduling client response is expanded with new fields, and some compile issues around tier type mappings in ticket sync services are resolved by adjusting DTO types."
  }
}
```

## Entry 1178

**Content:**
```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-03T10:25:24.145Z",
  "type": "event_msg",
  "payload": {
    "type": "agent_reasoning",
    "text": "**Adding new request type and refining exceptions**\n\nA new `PricingTierRequest` using `TierType` is introduced, and `ShowSlotService` will handle its list with null checks. Exception handling is expanded with alternatives in error responses, and a new `IntegrationException` is created for client errors. A Maven build is planned to catch compile issues before proceeding."
  }
}
```

    "type": "agent_reasoning",
    "text": "**Verifying module updates and compatibility**\n\nI confirmed no immediate changes needed for identity or exception subclasses despite new base constructor; updated shared SecurityConfig for callback path; ensured scheduling uses new Money embeddable for pricing tiers; updated services and mappers to handle pricing and date-time parameters correctly."
  }
}
```

## Entry 1221
**Timestamp:** 2026-03-03T10:26:53.373Z
**Role/Type:** event_msg
**Content:**
```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-03T10:26:53.373Z",
  "type": "event_msg",
  "payload": {
    "type": "agent_message",
    "message": "Summary:\n- Added Eventbrite OAuth token storage + ACL façade stubs in `shared/` (token store, DTOs, retry policy, shared events), plus `SeatingMode`, `Roles`, and enhanced error responses with alternatives support.\n- Extended `discovery-catalog` venues for EB sync status + reconciliation, added admin venue endpoints and slot-activation/cancellation listeners, and added migrations V5–V9.\n- Introduced new modules `scheduling`, `booking-inventory`, and `admin` with slot lifecycle, ticket sync listener, OAuth endpoints, and admin dashboard aggregation.\n\nNotable deviations from SPEC_2:\n- Published cross-module events under `shared/common/event/published` to avoid circular module dependencies.\n- Admin venue aggregation exposed at `/api/v1/admin/dashboard/venues/flagged` to avoid path collision with `discovery-catalog`’s `/api/v1/admin/catalog/venues/flagged`.\n- Org context is currently taken from `orgId` query params on admin/scheduling endpoints (JWT claim resolution not implemented).\n\nTests:\n- `mvn -q -pl app -am -DskipTests package`\n\nFiles to review (high-impact):\n- `shared/src/main/java/com/eventplatform/shared/eventbrite/service/EbTokenStore.java`\n- `discovery-catalog/src/main/java/com/eventplatform/discoverycatalog/service/VenueService.java`\n- `scheduling/src/main/java/com/eventplatform/scheduling/service/ShowSlotService.java`\n- `booking-inventory/src/main/java/com/eventplatform/bookinginventory/service/SlotTicketSyncService.java`\n- `admin/src/main/java/com/eventplatform/admin/api/controller/OrgEventbriteController.java`\n- `app/src/main/resources/db/migration/V5__alter_venue_table.sql`\n- `app/src/main/resources/db/migration/V6__create_organization_auth_table.sql`\n- `app/src/main/resources/db/migration/V7__create_show_slot_table.sql`\n- `app/src/main/resources/db/migration/V8__create_show_slot_occurrence_table.sql`\n- `app/src/main/resources/db/migration/V9__create_show_slot_pricing_tier_table.sql`\n\nIf you want, I can follow up with:\n1. Aligning org resolution to JWT claims (remove `orgId` query params).\n2. Implementing proper Eventbrite HTTP clients (OAuth + venues/events/tickets).\n3. Reconciling admin venue aggregator path to match SPEC_2 exactly.",
    "phase": null
  }
}
```

Aligning org resolution to JWT claims (remove `orgId` query params).\n2. Implementing proper Eventbrite HTTP clients (OAuth + venues/events/tickets).\n3. Reconciling admin venue aggregator path to match SPEC_2 exactly."
      }
    ]
  }
}

{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-03T10:26:53.550Z",
  "type": "event_msg",
  "payload": {
    "type": "task_complete",
    "turn_id": "019cb319-24b3-7901-a759-816ba7e2734f",
    "last_agent_message": "Summary:\n- Added Eventbrite OAuth token storage + ACL façade stubs in `shared/` (token store, DTOs, retry policy, shared events), plus `SeatingMode`, `Roles`, and enhanced error responses with alternatives support.\n- Extended `discovery-catalog` venues for EB sync status + reconciliation, added admin venue endpoints and slot-activation/cancellation listeners, and added migrations V5–V9.\n- Introduced new modules `scheduling`, `booking-inventory`, and `admin` with slot lifecycle, ticket sync listener, OAuth endpoints, and admin dashboard aggregation.\n\nNotable deviations from SPEC_2:\n- Published cross-module events under `shared/common/event/published` to avoid circular module dependencies.\n- Admin venue aggregation exposed at `/api/v1/admin/dashboard/venues/flagged` to avoid path collision with `discovery-catalog`’s `/api/v1/admin/catalog/venues/flagged`.\n- Org context is currently taken from `orgId` query params on admin/scheduling endpoints (JWT claim resolution not implemented).\n\nTests:\n- `mvn -q -pl app -am -DskipTests package`\n\nFiles to review (high-impact):\n- `shared/src/main/java/com/eventplatform/shared/eventbrite/service/EbTokenStore.java`\n- `discovery-catalog/src/main/java/com/eventplatform/discoverycatalog/service/VenueService.java`\n- `scheduling/src/main/java/com/eventplatform/scheduling/service/ShowSlotService.java`\n- `booking-inventory/src/main/java/com/eventplatform/bookinginventory/service/SlotTicketSyncService.java`\n- `admin/src/main/java/com/eventplatform/admin/api/controller/OrgEventbriteController.java`\n- `app/src/main/resources/db/migration/V5__alter_venue_table.sql`\n- `app/src/main/resources/db/migration/V6__create_organization_auth_table.sql`\n- `app/src/main/resources/db/migration/V7__create_show_slot_table.sql`\n- `app/src/main/resources/db/migration/V8__create_show_slot_occurrence_table.sql`\n- `app/src/main/resources/db/migration/V9__create_show_slot_pricing_tier_table.sql`\n\nIf you want, I can follow up with:\n1. Aligning org resolution to JWT claims (remove `orgId` query params).\n2. Implementing proper Eventbrite HTTP clients (OAuth + venues/events/tickets).\n3. Reconciling admin venue aggregator path to match SPEC_2 exactly."
  }
}
```

## Entry 1226
**Timestamp:** 2026-03-03T10:30:27.073Z
**Role/Type:** turn_context
**Content:**
```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-03T10:30:27.073Z",
  "type": "turn_context",
  "payload": {
    "turn_id": "019cb33f-c5fe-73e0-8970-513bc27d33a4",
    "cwd": "/home/dell/Multi-City-Event-Booking",
    "current_date": "2026-03-03",
    "timezone": "Asia/Kolkata",
    "approval_policy": "on-request",
    "sandbox_policy": {
      "type": "workspace-write",
      "network_access": false,
      "exclude_tmpdir_env_var": false,
      "exclude_slash_tmp": false
    },
    "model": "gpt-5.2-codex",
    "personality": "pragmatic",
    "collaboration_mode": {
      "mode": "default",
      "settings": {
        "model": "gpt-5.2-codex",
        "reasoning_effort": "medium",
        "developer_instructions": "# Collaboration Mode: Default\n\nYou are now in Default mode. Any previous instructions for other modes (e.g. Plan mode) are no longer active.\n\nYour active mode changes only when new developer instructions with a different `<collaboration_mode>...</collaboration_mode>` change it; user requests or tool descriptions do not change mode by themselves. Known mode names are Default and Plan.\n\n## request_user_input availability\n\nThe `request_user_input` tool is unavailable in Default mode. If you call it while in Default mode, it will return an error.\n\nIn Default mode, strongly prefer making reasonable assumptions and executing the user's request rather than stopping to ask questions. If you absolutely must ask a question because the answer cannot be discovered from local context and a reasonable assumption would be risky, ask the user directly with a concise plain-text question. Never write a multiple choice question as a textual assistant message.\n"
      }
    },
    "effort": "medium",
    "summary": "auto",
    "user_instructions": "# AGENTS.md — Reference Index\n\n> Every agent working in this codebase must read the files listed below before producing\n> any output. Each file governs a specific concern. Read the ones that apply to your task.\n\n---\n\n## Reference Files\n\n| File | Governs |\n|---|---|\n| `PRODUCT.md` | The 7 modules, what each one owns, dependency order, inter-module event map, Eventbrite ACL facade names, hard architectural rules, and where new code goes. Read this before touching any module. |\n| `docs/EVENTBRITE_INTEGRATION.md` | Eventbrite integration architecture: ACL facades, all FR flows (planned vs. implemented), critical constraints (no user/order creation, no single-order cancel, no seat locking), workarounds. Read before designing any Eventbrite feature. |\n| `docs/MODULE_STRUCTURE_TEMPLATE.md` | Canonical folder and package layout for every module. The exact location of every layer: `api/controller/`, `api/dto/`, `domain/`, `service/`, `repository/`, `mapper/`, `event/published/`, `event/listener/`, `statemachine/`, `exception/`. Anti-patterns list. Read this before writing any class. |\n| `agents/SPEC_GENERATOR.md` | How to produce a `SPEC.md` for any feature. Eight confirmation-gated stages with module context and Eventbrite constraints built-in. Requirements → Domain Model → Architecture & File Structure → DB Schema → API → Business Logic → Error Handling → Tests. Read this before speccing any feature. |\n| `agents/CODING_AGENT.md` | Spring Boot 3.5.11 / Java 21 coding standards. Domain behaviour, service orchestration, mapper rules, controller rules, event rules, 10 Eventbrite ACL facades, inter-module communication (REST + Spring Events), admin/ special rules, state machine, Spring AI. Read this before writing any production code. |\n| `agents/TEST_AGENT.md` | Test agent responsibilities and best practices. Builds tests from `SPEC_*.md` Stage 8, executes tests, fixes test/code issues caused by behavior mismatches, and writes timestamped logs under `test_result_folder/`. Read this before writing or running tests. |\n| `docs/TESTING_GUIDE.md` | Four-layer test structure: `domain/` (unit), `service/` (Mockito), `api/` (@WebMvcTest), `arch/` (ArchUnit enforcing 10 hard rules + Eventbrite constraints). All rule examples, happy path, negative path, exception patterns. Read before writing any test. |\n| `mock-eventbrite-api/README.md` | **Mock Eventbrite Service:** Python FastAPI in-memory mock of Eventbrite API v3. Used for local development and testing. Exposes all 10 ACL facade endpoints without hitting production Eventbrite. See setup instructions below. |\n\n---\n\n## Module Quick Reference\n\nFull detail for each module is in `PRODUCT.md`. This table is a lookup only.\n\n| Module | Owns | Eventbrite ACL Facades Used |\n|---|---|---|\n| `discovery-catalog` | Events, Venues, Cities, catalog sync | `EbEventSyncService`, `EbTicketService` |\n| `scheduling` | Show slots, conflict validation | `EbEventSyncService` |\n| `identity` | Users, JWT, profiles, preferences, wallet | `EbAttendeeService` |\n| `booking-inventory` | Seats, SeatMap, Cart, Pricing, Seat Lock State Machine | `EbTicketService` |\n| `payments-ticketing` | Bookings, Payments, E-Tickets, Cancellations, Refunds, Wallet | `EbOrderService`, `EbAttendeeService`, `EbRefundService` |\n| `promotions` | Coupons, Promotions, Eligibility rules | `EbDiscountSyncService` |\n| `engagement` | Reviews, Moderation, AI Chatbot (`service/chatbot/`), RAG pipeline | `EbEventSyncService` |\n| `admin/` | No domain ownership. Orchestration and aggregation only. | — |\n| `shared/` | All external ACL facades, JWT, base classes, common DTOs, configs | — |\n\n---\n\n## Hard Rules — Never Violate\n\nCanonical numbering and wording are owned by `PRODUCT.md` Section 9. These shorthand rules apply to every task without exception.\n\n1. No module imports another module's `@Service`, `@Entity`, or `@Repository`.\n  - Exception: `admin/` MAY import module `@Service` beans for READ operations only; all writes MUST go through the owning module REST API.\n2. No module calls Eventbrite, Razorpay, or OpenAI HTTP directly — only through `shared/` ACL facades.\n3. ACL facade names are exactly as listed below — never invent new ones.\n4. Spring Events carry only primitives, IDs, enums, and value objects — never `@Entity` objects.\n5. One `GlobalExceptionHandler` in `shared/common/exception/` — never `@ControllerAdvice` in a module.\n6. All field mapping goes in `mapper/` using MapStruct — never inside a service or controller.\n7. `admin/` owns no `@Entity` and no database table.\n8. `shared/` has no dependency on any module.\n9. `app/` has zero business logic.\n10. Dependency direction is strictly downward — no circular imports between modules.\n\n---\n\n## Eventbrite ACL Facade Names (HARD RULE #3)\nRead the docs/eventbriteapiv3public.apib for detail API Endpoint with schema, Response, Request and errors\n\nAll 10 facades are in `shared/eventbrite/service/`. These are the ONLY things modules call for Eventbrite:\n\n1. **EbEventSyncService** — Create, update, publish, cancel events; pull org event catalog\n2. **EbVenueService** — Create and update venues; list org venues\n3. **EbScheduleService** — Create recurring event schedules and occurrences\n4. **EbTicketService** — Create/update ticket classes; manage inventory; check availability\n5. **EbCapacityService** — Retrieve and update event capacity tiers\n6. **EbOrderService** — Read orders post-checkout (NOT creation — JS SDK widget only)\n7. **EbAttendeeService** — List attendees; verify attendance for reviews\n8. **EbDiscountSyncService** — Create, update, delete discount codes\n9. **EbRefundService** — Read refund status only (NO submission API — workaround required)\n10. **EbWebhookService** — Mock/testing only; not used in production registration flow\n\n**See `docs/EVENTBRITE_INTEGRATION.md` for which module uses which facade and what gaps exist.**\n\n---\n\n## Module Dependency Order (HARD RULE #10)\n\n```\nshared/               <- foundational; no dependencies\n  |\n  v\ndiscovery-catalog    <- depends on shared only\n  |\n  v\nscheduling           <- depends on shared + discovery-catalog (REST reads)\nidentity             <- depends on shared only\n  |\n  v\nbooking-inventory    <- depends on shared + discovery-catalog (REST reads)\n  |\n  v\npayments-ticketing   <- depends on shared + booking-inventory + identity (REST)\n  |\n  v\npromotions           <- depends on shared + booking-inventory + identity (REST)\n  |\n  v\nengagement           <- depends on shared + payments-ticketing + identity (REST)\n  |\n  v\nadmin/               <- thin orchestration; reads from all, writes via REST APIs\n  |\n  v\napp/                 <- entry point only; zero business logic\n```\n\n**Golden Rule:** Module at level N can depend on level N-1 or lower (or `shared/`) only. Never import from sibling or downstream modules.\n\n**See `PRODUCT.md` Section 2 for detailed rationale.**\n\n\n## Skills\nA skill is a set of local instructions to follow that is stored in a `SKILL.md` file. Below is the list of skills that can be used. Each entry includes a name, description, and file path so you can open the source for full instructions when using a specific skill.\n### Available skills\n- find-skills: Helps users discover and install agent skills when they ask questions like \"how do I do X\", \"find a skill for X\", \"is there a skill that can...\", or express interest in extending capabilities. This skill should be used when the user is looking for functionality that might exist as an installable skill. (file: /home/dell/.agents/skills/find-skills/SKILL.md)\n- skill-creator: Guide for creating effective skills. This skill should be used when users want to create a new skill (or update an existing skill) that extends Codex's capabilities with specialized knowledge, workflows, or tool integrations. (file: /home/dell/.codex/skills/.system/skill-creator/SKILL.md)\n- skill-installer: Install Codex skills into $CODEX_HOME/skills from a curated list or a GitHub repo path. Use when a user asks to list installable skills, install a curated skill, or install a skill from another repo (including private repos). (file: /home/dell/.codex/skills/.system/skill-installer/SKILL.md)\n### How to use skills\n- Discovery: The list above is the skills available in this session (name + description + file path). Skill bodies live on disk at the listed paths.\n- Trigger rules: If the user names a skill (with `$SkillName` or plain text) OR the task clearly matches a skill's description shown above, you must use that skill for that turn. Multiple mentions mean use them all. Do not carry skills across turns unless re-mentioned.\n- Missing/blocked: If a named skill isn't in the list or the path can't be read, say so briefly and continue with the best fallback.\n- How to use a skill (progressive disclosure):\n  1) After deciding to use a skill, open its `SKILL.md`. Read only enough to follow the workflow.\n  2) When `SKILL.md` references relative paths (e.g., `scripts/foo.py`), resolve them relative to the skill directory listed above first, and only consider other paths if needed.\n  3) If `SKILL.md` points to extra folders such as `references/`, load only the specific files needed for the request; don't bulk-load everything.\n  4) If `scripts/` exist, prefer running or patching them instead of retyping large code blocks.\n  5) If `assets/` or templates exist, reuse them instead of recreating from scratch.\n- Coordination and sequencing:\n  - If multiple skills apply, choose the minimal set that covers the request and state the order you'll use them.\n  - Announce which skill(s) you're using and why (one short line). If you skip an obvious skill, say why.\n- Context hygiene:\n  - Keep context small: summarize long sections instead of pasting them; only load extra files when needed.\n  - Avoid deep reference-chasing: prefer opening only files directly linked from `SKILL.md` unless you're blocked.\n  - When variants exist (frameworks, providers, domains), pick only the relevant reference file(s) and note that choice.\n- Safety and fallback: If a skill can't be applied cleanly (missing files, unclear instructions), state the issue, pick the next-best approach, and continue.",
    "truncation_policy": {
      "mode": "tokens",
      "limit": 10000
    }
  }
}
```

No module calls Eventbrite, Razorpay, or OpenAI HTTP directly — only through `shared/` ACL facades.\n3. ACL facade names are exactly as listed below — never invent new ones.\n4. Spring Events carry only primitives, IDs, enums, and value objects — never `@Entity` objects.\n5. One `GlobalExceptionHandler` in `shared/common/exception/` — never `@ControllerAdvice` in a module.\n6. All field mapping goes in `mapper/` using MapStruct — never inside a service or controller.\n7. `admin/` owns no `@Entity` and no database table.\n8. `shared/` has no dependency on any module.\n9. `app/` has zero business logic.\n10. Dependency direction is strictly downward — no circular imports between modules.\n\n---\n\n## Eventbrite ACL Facade Names (HARD RULE #3)\nRead the docs/eventbriteapiv3public.apib for detail API Endpoint with schema, Response, Request and errors\n\nAll 10 facades are in `shared/eventbrite/service/`. These are the ONLY things modules call for Eventbrite:\n\n1. **EbEventSyncService** — Create, update, publish, cancel events; pull org event catalog\n2. **EbVenueService** — Create and update venues; list org venues\n3. **EbScheduleService** — Create recurring event schedules and occurrences\n4. **EbTicketService** — Create/update ticket classes; manage inventory; check availability\n5. **EbCapacityService** — Retrieve and update event capacity tiers\n6. **EbOrderService** — Read orders post-checkout (NOT creation — JS SDK widget only)\n7. **EbAttendeeService** — List attendees; verify attendance for reviews\n8. **EbDiscountSyncService** — Create, update, delete discount codes\n9. **EbRefundService** — Read refund status only (NO submission API — workaround required)\n10. **EbWebhookService** — Mock/testing only; not used in production registration flow\n\n**See `docs/EVENTBRITE_INTEGRATION.md` for which module uses which facade and what gaps exist.**\n\n---\n\n## Module Dependency Order (HARD RULE #10)\n\n```\nshared/               <- foundational; no dependencies\n  |\n  v\ndiscovery-catalog    <- depends on shared only\n  |\n  v\nscheduling           <- depends on shared + discovery-catalog (REST reads)\nidentity             <- depends on shared only\n  |\n  v\nbooking-inventory    <- depends on shared + discovery-catalog (REST reads)\n  |\n  v\npayments-ticketing   <- depends on shared + booking-inventory + identity (REST)\n  |\n  v\npromotions           <- depends on shared + booking-inventory + identity (REST)\n  |\n  v\nengagement           <- depends on shared + payments-ticketing + identity (REST)\n  |\n  v\nadmin/               <- thin orchestration; reads from all, writes via REST APIs\n  |\n  v\napp/                 <- entry point only; zero business logic\n```\n\n**Golden Rule:** Module at level N can depend on level N-1 or lower (or `shared/`) only. Never import from sibling or downstream modules.\n\n**See `PRODUCT.md` Section 2 for detailed rationale.**\n\n\n## Skills\nA skill is a set of local instructions to follow that is stored in a `SKILL.md` file. Below is the list of skills that can be used. Each entry includes a name, description, and file path so you can open the source for full instructions when using a specific skill.\n### Available skills\n- find-skills: Helps users discover and install agent skills when they ask questions like \"how do I do X\", \"find a skill for X\", \"is there a skill that can...\", or express interest in extending capabilities. This skill should be used when the user is looking for functionality that might exist as an installable skill. (file: /home/dell/.agents/skills/find-skills/SKILL.md)\n- skill-creator: Guide for creating effective skills. This skill should be used when users want to create a new skill (or update an existing skill) that extends Codex's capabilities with specialized knowledge, workflows, or tool integrations. (file: /home/dell/.codex/skills/.system/skill-creator/SKILL.md)\n- skill-installer: Install Codex skills into $CODEX_HOME/skills from a curated list or a GitHub repo path. Use when a user asks to list installable skills, install a curated skill, or install a skill from another repo (including private repos). (file: /home/dell/.codex/skills/.system/skill-installer/SKILL.md)\n### How to use skills\n- Discovery: The list above is the skills available in this session (name + description + file path). Skill bodies live on disk at the listed paths.\n- Trigger rules: If the user names a skill (with `$SkillName` or plain text) OR the task clearly matches a skill's description shown above, you must use that skill for that turn. Multiple mentions mean use them all. Do not carry skills across turns unless re-mentioned.\n- Missing/blocked: If a named skill isn't in the list or the path can't be read, say so briefly and continue with the best fallback.\n- How to use a skill (progressive disclosure):\n  1) After deciding to use a skill, open its `SKILL.md`. Read only enough to follow the workflow.\n  2) When `SKILL.md` references relative paths (e.g., `scripts/foo.py`), resolve them relative to the skill directory listed above first, and only consider other paths if needed.\n  3) If `SKILL.md` points to extra folders such as `references/`, load only the specific files needed for the request; don't bulk-load everything.\n  4) If `scripts/` exist, prefer running or patching them instead of retyping large code blocks.\n  5) If `assets/` or templates exist, reuse them instead of recreating from scratch.\n- Coordination and sequencing:\n  - If multiple skills apply, choose the minimal set that covers the request and state the order you'll use them.\n  - Announce which skill(s) you're using and why (one short line). If you skip an obvious skill, say why.\n- Context hygiene:\n  - Keep context small: summarize long sections instead of pasting them; only load extra files when needed.\n  - Avoid deep reference-chasing: prefer opening only files directly linked from `SKILL.md` unless you're blocked.\n  - When variants exist (frameworks, providers, domains), pick only the relevant reference file(s) and note that choice.\n- Safety and fallback: If a skill can't be applied cleanly (missing files, unclear instructions), state the issue, pick the next-best approach, and continue.",
    "truncation_policy": {
      "mode": "tokens",
      "limit": 10000
    }
  }
}
```

## Entry 1229
**Timestamp:** 2026-03-03T10:30:37.117Z
**Role/Type:** event_msg
**Content:**
```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-03T10:30:37.117Z",
  "type": "event_msg",
  "payload": {
    "type": "agent_message",
    "message": "**Brief follow-ups**\n\n1) **JWT org resolution (remove `orgId` params)**  \n- Goal: Derive `orgId` from authenticated admin JWT claim everywhere.  \n- Work: Add JWT claim parsing helper (likely in `shared/security`), update controllers/services in `admin/`, `scheduling/`, `discovery-catalog/` to stop accepting `orgId` query param.  \n- Risk: Requires JWT structure to be confirmed (claim name, type).  \n\n2) **Eventbrite HTTP clients**  \n- Goal: Replace stub ACLs with real REST clients for OAuth, venue, event, ticket, capacity, schedule.  \n- Work: Add WebClient/RestClient in `shared/eventbrite/client/`, implement `EbOAuthClient`, `EbVenueService`, `EbEventSyncService`, `EbTicketService`, `EbCapacityService`, `EbScheduleService`. Use `EbTokenStore` to inject bearer tokens; apply retry policy and `Retry-After` handling.  \n- Risk: Contract accuracy with EB API v3; needs API credentials and base URL config.  \n\n3) **Admin venue aggregator path alignment**  \n- Goal: Match SPEC_2 endpoint exactly (`GET /api/v1/admin/catalog/venues/flagged` for admin aggregation).  \n- Work: Either move admin aggregator to `/api/v1/admin/catalog/venues/flagged` and call discovery-catalog at a different internal path, or keep discovery-catalog path and add `/api/v1/admin/venues/flagged` as internal-only.  \n- Risk: Endpoint collision if both modules expose the same path in a single app context.  \n\nIf you want, pick 1–3 and I’ll start.",
    "phase": null
  }
}
```

**Content:**
```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-03T10:32:16.960Z",
  "type": "turn_context",
  "payload": {
    "turn_id": "019cb341-733e-7cd2-bbdc-3e04324b1210",
    "cwd": "/home/dell/Multi-City-Event-Booking",
    "current_date": "2026-03-03",
    "timezone": "Asia/Kolkata",
    "approval_policy": "on-request",
    "sandbox_policy": {
      "type": "workspace-write",
      "network_access": false,
      "exclude_tmpdir_env_var": false,
      "exclude_slash_tmp": false
    },
    "model": "gpt-5.2-codex",
    "personality": "pragmatic",
    "collaboration_mode": {
      "mode": "default",
      "settings": {
        "model": "gpt-5.2-codex",
        "reasoning_effort": "medium",
        "developer_instructions": "# Collaboration Mode: Default\n\nYou are now in Default mode. Any previous instructions for other modes (e.g. Plan mode) are no longer active.\n\nYour active mode changes only when new developer instructions with a different `<collaboration_mode>...</collaboration_mode>` change it; user requests or tool descriptions do not change mode by themselves. Known mode names are Default and Plan.\n\n## request_user_input availability\n\nThe `request_user_input` tool is unavailable in Default mode. If you call it while in Default mode, it will return an error.\n\nIn Default mode, strongly prefer making reasonable assumptions and executing the user's request rather than stopping to ask questions. If you absolutely must ask a question because the answer cannot be discovered from local context and a reasonable assumption would be risky, ask the user directly with a concise plain-text question. Never write a multiple choice question as a textual assistant message.\n"
      }
    },
    "effort": "medium",
    "summary": "auto",
    "user_instructions": "# AGENTS.md — Reference Index\n\n> Every agent working in this codebase must read the files listed below before producing\n> any output. Each file governs a specific concern. Read the ones that apply to your task.\n\n---\n\n## Reference Files\n\n| File | Governs |\n|---|---|\n| `PRODUCT.md` | The 7 modules, what each one owns, dependency order, inter-module event map, Eventbrite ACL facade names, hard architectural rules, and where new code goes. Read this before touching any module. |\n| `docs/EVENTBRITE_INTEGRATION.md` | Eventbrite integration architecture: ACL facades, all FR flows (planned vs. implemented), critical constraints (no user/order creation, no single-order cancel, no seat locking), workarounds. Read before designing any Eventbrite feature. |\n| `docs/MODULE_STRUCTURE_TEMPLATE.md` | Canonical folder and package layout for every module. The exact location of every layer: `api/controller/`, `api/dto/`, `domain/`, `service/`, `repository/`, `mapper/`, `event/published/`, `event/listener/`, `statemachine/`, `exception/`. Anti-patterns list. Read this before writing any class. |\n| `agents/SPEC_GENERATOR.md` | How to produce a `SPEC.md` for any feature. Eight confirmation-gated stages with module context and Eventbrite constraints built-in. Requirements → Domain Model → Architecture & File Structure → DB Schema → API → Business Logic → Error Handling → Tests. Read this before speccing any feature. |\n| `agents/CODING_AGENT.md` | Spring Boot 3.5.11 / Java 21 coding standards. Domain behaviour, service orchestration, mapper rules, controller rules, event rules, 10 Eventbrite ACL facades, inter-module communication (REST + Spring Events), admin/ special rules, state machine, Spring AI. Read this before writing any production code. |\n| `agents/TEST_AGENT.md` | Test agent responsibilities and best practices. Builds tests from `SPEC_*.md` Stage 8, executes tests, fixes test/code issues caused by behavior mismatches, and writes timestamped logs under `test_result_folder/`. Read this before writing or running tests. |\n| `docs/TESTING_GUIDE.md` | Four-layer test structure: `domain/` (unit), `service/` (Mockito), `api/` (@WebMvcTest), `arch/` (ArchUnit enforcing 10 hard rules + Eventbrite constraints). All rule examples, happy path, negative path, exception patterns. Read before writing any test. |\n| `mock-eventbrite-api/README.md` | **Mock Eventbrite Service:** Python FastAPI in-memory mock of Eventbrite API v3. Used for local development and testing. Exposes all 10 ACL facade endpoints without hitting production Eventbrite. See setup instructions below. |\n\n---\n\n## Module Quick Reference\n\nFull detail for each module is in `PRODUCT.md`. This table is a lookup only.\n\n| Module | Owns | Eventbrite ACL Facades Used |\n|---|---|---|\n| `discovery-catalog` | Events, Venues, Cities, catalog sync | `EbEventSyncService`, `EbTicketService` |\n| `scheduling` | Show slots, conflict validation | `EbEventSyncService` |\n| `identity` | Users, JWT, profiles, preferences, wallet | `EbAttendeeService` |\n| `booking-inventory` | Seats, SeatMap, Cart, Pricing, Seat Lock State Machine | `EbTicketService` |\n| `payments-ticketing` | Bookings, Payments, E-Tickets, Cancellations, Refunds, Wallet | `EbOrderService`, `EbAttendeeService`, `EbRefundService` |\n| `promotions` | Coupons, Promotions, Eligibility rules | `EbDiscountSyncService` |\n| `engagement` | Reviews, Moderation, AI Chatbot (`service/chatbot/`), RAG pipeline | `EbEventSyncService` |\n| `admin/` | No domain ownership. Orchestration and aggregation only. | — |\n| `shared/` | All external ACL facades, JWT, base classes, common DTOs, configs | — |\n\n---\n\n## Hard Rules — Never Violate\n\nCanonical numbering and wording are owned by `PRODUCT.md` Section 9. These shorthand rules apply to every task without exception.\n\n1. No module imports another module's `@Service`, `@Entity`, or `@Repository`.\n  - Exception: `admin/` MAY import module `@Service` beans for READ operations only; all writes MUST go through the owning module REST API.\n2. No module calls Eventbrite, Razorpay, or OpenAI HTTP directly — only through `shared/` ACL facades.\n3. ACL facade names are exactly as listed below — never invent new ones.\n4. Spring Events carry only primitives, IDs, enums, and value objects — never `@Entity` objects.\n5. One `GlobalExceptionHandler` in `shared/common/exception/` — never `@ControllerAdvice` in a module.\n6. All field mapping goes in `mapper/` using MapStruct — never inside a service or controller.\n7. `admin/` owns no `@Entity` and no database table.\n8. `shared/` has no dependency on any module.\n9. `app/` has zero business logic.\n10. Dependency direction is strictly downward — no circular imports between modules.\n\n---\n\n## Eventbrite ACL Facade Names (HARD RULE #3)\nRead the docs/eventbriteapiv3public.apib for detail API Endpoint with schema, Response, Request and errors\n\nAll 10 facades are in `shared/eventbrite/service/`. These are the ONLY things modules call for Eventbrite:\n\n1. **EbEventSyncService** — Create, update, publish, cancel events; pull org event catalog\n2. **EbVenueService** — Create and update venues; list org venues\n3. **EbScheduleService** — Create recurring event schedules and occurrences\n4. **EbTicketService** — Create/update ticket classes; manage inventory; check availability\n5. **EbCapacityService** — Retrieve and update event capacity tiers\n6. **EbOrderService** — Read orders post-checkout (NOT creation — JS SDK widget only)\n7. **EbAttendeeService** — List attendees; verify attendance for reviews\n8. **EbDiscountSyncService** — Create, update, delete discount codes\n9. **EbRefundService** — Read refund status only (NO submission API — workaround required)\n10. **EbWebhookService** — Mock/testing only; not used in production registration flow\n\n**See `docs/EVENTBRITE_INTEGRATION.md` for which module uses which facade and what gaps exist.**\n\n---\n\n## Module Dependency Order (HARD RULE #10)\n\n```\nshared/               <- foundational; no dependencies\n  |\n  v\ndiscovery-catalog    <- depends on shared only\n  |\n  v\nscheduling           <- depends on shared + discovery-catalog (REST reads)\nidentity             <- depends on shared only\n  |\n  v\nbooking-inventory    <- depends on shared + discovery-catalog (REST reads)\n  |\n  v\npayments-ticketing   <- depends on shared + booking-inventory + identity (REST)\n  |\n  v\npromotions           <- depends on shared + booking-inventory + identity (REST)\n  |\n  v\nengagement           <- depends on shared + payments-ticketing + identity (REST)\n  |\n  v\nadmin/               <- thin orchestration; reads from all, writes via REST APIs\n  |\n  v\napp/                 <- entry point only; zero business logic\n```\n\n**Golden Rule:** Module at level N can depend on level N-1 or lower (or `shared/`) only. Never import from sibling or downstream modules.\n\n**See `PRODUCT.md` Section 2 for detailed rationale.**\n\n\n## Skills\nA skill is a set of local instructions to follow that is stored in a `SKILL.md` file. Below is the list of skills that can be used. Each entry includes a name, description, and file path so you can open the source for full instructions when using a specific skill.\n### Available skills\n- find-skills: Helps users discover and install agent skills when they ask questions like \"how do I do X\", \"find a skill for X\", \"is there a skill that can...\", or express interest in extending capabilities. This skill should be used when the user is looking for functionality that might exist as an installable skill. (file: /home/dell/.agents/skills/find-skills/SKILL.md)\n- skill-creator: Guide for creating effective skills. This skill should be used when users want to create a new skill (or update an existing skill) that extends Codex's capabilities with specialized knowledge, workflows, or tool integrations. (file: /home/dell/.codex/skills/.system/skill-creator/SKILL.md)\n- skill-installer: Install Codex skills into $CODEX_HOME/skills from a curated list or a GitHub repo path. Use when a user asks to list installable skills, install a curated skill, or install a skill from another repo (including private repos). (file: /home/dell/.codex/skills/.system/skill-installer/SKILL.md)\n### How to use skills\n- Discovery: The list above is the skills available in this session (name + description + file path). Skill bodies live on disk at the listed paths.\n- Trigger rules: If the user names a skill (with `$SkillName` or plain text) OR the task clearly matches a skill's description shown above, you must use that skill for that turn. Multiple mentions mean use them all. Do not carry skills across turns unless re-mentioned.\n- Missing/blocked: If a named skill isn't in the list or the path can't be read, say so briefly and continue with the best fallback.\n- How to use a skill (progressive disclosure):\n  1) After deciding to use a skill, open its `SKILL.md`. Read only enough to follow the workflow.\n  2) When `SKILL.md` references relative paths (e.g., `scripts/foo.py`), resolve them relative to the skill directory listed above first, and only consider other paths if needed.\n  3) If `SKILL.md` points to extra folders such as `references/`, load only the specific files needed for the request; don't bulk-load everything.\n  4) If `scripts/` exist, prefer running or patching them instead of retyping large code blocks.\n  5) If `assets/` or templates exist, reuse them instead of recreating from scratch.\n- Coordination and sequencing:\n  - If multiple skills apply, choose the minimal set that covers the request and state the order you'll use them.\n  - Announce which skill(s) you're using and why (one short line). If you skip an obvious skill, say why.\n- Context hygiene:\n  - Keep context small: summarize long sections instead of pasting them; only load extra files when needed.\n  - Avoid deep reference-chasing: prefer opening only files directly linked from `SKILL.md` unless you're blocked.\n  - When variants exist (frameworks, providers, domains), pick only the relevant reference file(s) and note that choice.\n- Safety and fallback: If a skill can't be applied cleanly (missing files, unclear instructions), state the issue, pick the next-best approach, and continue.",

      "limit": 10000
    }

}

## Entry 1308
**Timestamp:** 2026-03-03T10:34:38.415Z
**Role/Type:** turn_context
**Content:**
```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-03T10:34:38.415Z",
  "type": "turn_context",
  "payload": {
    "turn_id": "019cb343-9bcd-7dc1-955f-c81ea357a83a",
    "cwd": "/home/dell/Multi-City-Event-Booking",
    "current_date": "2026-03-03",
    "timezone": "Asia/Kolkata",
    "approval_policy": "on-request",
    "sandbox_policy": {
      "type": "workspace-write",
      "network_access": false,
      "exclude_tmpdir_env_var": false,
      "exclude_slash_tmp": false
    },
    "model": "gpt-5.2-codex",
    "personality": "pragmatic",
    "collaboration_mode": {
      "mode": "default",
      "settings": {
        "model": "gpt-5.2-codex",
        "reasoning_effort": "medium",
        "developer_instructions": "# Collaboration Mode: Default\n\nYou are now in Default mode. Any previous instructions for other modes (e.g. Plan mode) are no longer active.\n\nYour active mode changes only when new developer instructions with a different `<collaboration_mode>...</collaboration_mode>` change it; user requests or tool descriptions do not change mode by themselves. Known mode names are Default and Plan.\n\n## request_user_input availability\n\nThe `request_user_input` tool is unavailable in Default mode. If you call it while in Default mode, it will return an error.\n\nIn Default mode, strongly prefer making reasonable assumptions and executing the user's request rather than stopping to ask questions. If you absolutely must ask a question because the answer cannot be discovered from local context and a reasonable assumption would be risky, ask the user directly with a concise plain-text question. Never write a multiple choice question as a textual assistant message.\n"
      }
    },
    "effort": "medium",
    "summary": "auto",
    "user_instructions": "# AGENTS.md — Reference Index\n\n> Every agent working in this codebase must read the files listed below before producing\n> any output. Each file governs a specific concern. Read the ones that apply to your task.\n\n---\n\n## Reference Files\n\n| File | Governs |\n|---|---|\n| `PRODUCT.md` | The 7 modules, what each one owns, dependency order, inter-module event map, Eventbrite ACL facade names, hard architectural rules, and where new code goes. Read this before touching any module. |\n| `docs/EVENTBRITE_INTEGRATION.md` | Eventbrite integration architecture: ACL facades, all FR flows (planned vs. implemented), critical constraints (no user/order creation, no single-order cancel, no seat locking), workarounds. Read before designing any Eventbrite feature. |\n| `docs/MODULE_STRUCTURE_TEMPLATE.md` | Canonical folder and package layout for every module. The exact location of every layer: `api/controller/`, `api/dto/`, `domain/`, `service/`, `repository/`, `mapper/`, `event/published/`, `event/listener/`, `statemachine/`, `exception/`. Anti-patterns list. Read this before writing any class. |\n| `agents/SPEC_GENERATOR.md` | How to produce a `SPEC.md` for any feature. Eight confirmation-gated stages with module context and Eventbrite constraints built-in. Requirements → Domain Model → Architecture & File Structure → DB Schema → API → Business Logic → Error Handling → Tests. Read this before speccing any feature. |\n| `agents/CODING_AGENT.md` | Spring Boot 3.5.11 / Java 21 coding standards. Domain behaviour, service orchestration, mapper rules, controller rules, event rules, 10 Eventbrite ACL facades, inter-module communication (REST + Spring Events), admin/ special rules, state machine, Spring AI. Read this before writing any production code. |\n| `agents/TEST_AGENT.md` | Test agent responsibilities and best practices. Builds tests from `SPEC_*.md` Stage 8, executes tests, fixes test/code issues caused by behavior mismatches, and writes timestamped logs under `test_result_folder/`. Read this before writing or running tests. |\n| `docs/TESTING_GUIDE.md` | Four-layer test structure: `domain/` (unit), `service/` (Mockito), `api/` (@WebMvcTest), `arch/` (ArchUnit enforcing 10 hard rules + Eventbrite constraints). All rule examples, happy path, negative path, exception patterns. Read before writing any test. |\n| `mock-eventbrite-api/README.md` | **Mock Eventbrite Service:** Python FastAPI in-memory mock of Eventbrite API v3. Used for local development and testing. Exposes all 10 ACL facade endpoints without hitting production Eventbrite. See setup instructions below. |\n\n---\n\n## Module Quick Reference\n\nFull detail for each module is in `PRODUCT.md`. This table is a lookup only.\n\n| Module | Owns | Eventbrite ACL Facades Used |\n|---|---|---|\n| `discovery-catalog` | Events, Venues, Cities, catalog sync | `EbEventSyncService`, `EbTicketService` |\n| `scheduling` | Show slots, conflict validation | `EbEventSyncService` |\n| `identity` | Users, JWT, profiles, preferences, wallet | `EbAttendeeService` |\n| `booking-inventory` | Seats, SeatMap, Cart, Pricing, Seat Lock State Machine | `EbTicketService` |\n| `payments-ticketing` | Bookings, Payments, E-Tickets, Cancellations, Refunds, Wallet | `EbOrderService`, `EbAttendeeService`, `EbRefundService` |\n| `promotions` | Coupons, Promotions, Eligibility rules | `EbDiscountSyncService` |\n| `engagement` | Reviews, Moderation, AI Chatbot (`service/chatbot/`), RAG pipeline | `EbEventSyncService` |\n| `admin/` | No domain ownership. Orchestration and aggregation only. | — |\n| `shared/` | All external ACL facades, JWT, base classes, common DTOs, configs | — |\n\n---\n\n## Hard Rules — Never Violate\n\nCanonical numbering and wording are owned by `PRODUCT.md` Section 9. These shorthand rules apply to every task without exception.\n\n1. No module imports another module's `@Service`, `@Entity`, or `@Repository`.\n  - Exception: `admin/` MAY import module `@Service` beans for READ operations only; all writes MUST go through the owning module REST API.\n2. No module calls Eventbrite, Razorpay, or OpenAI HTTP directly — only through `shared/` ACL facades.\n3. ACL facade names are exactly as listed below — never invent new ones.\n4. Spring Events carry only primitives, IDs, enums, and value objects — never `@Entity` objects.\n5. One `GlobalExceptionHandler` in `shared/common/exception/` — never `@ControllerAdvice` in a module.\n6. All field mapping goes in `mapper/` using MapStruct — never inside a service or controller.\n7. `admin/` owns no `@Entity` and no database table.\n8. `shared/` has no dependency on any module.\n9. `app/` has zero business logic.\n10. Dependency direction is strictly downward — no circular imports between modules.\n\n---\n\n## Eventbrite ACL Facade Names (HARD RULE #3)\nRead the docs/eventbriteapiv3public.apib for detail API Endpoint with schema, Response, Request and errors\n\nAll 10 facades are in `shared/eventbrite/service/`. These are the ONLY things modules call for Eventbrite:\n\n1. **EbEventSyncService** — Create, update, publish, cancel events; pull org event catalog\n2. **EbVenueService** — Create and update venues; list org venues\n3. **EbScheduleService** — Create recurring event schedules and occurrences\n4. **EbTicketService** — Create/update ticket classes; manage inventory; check availability\n5. **EbCapacityService** — Retrieve and update event capacity tiers\n6. **EbOrderService** — Read orders post-checkout (NOT creation — JS SDK widget only)\n7. **EbAttendeeService** — List attendees; verify attendance for reviews\n8. **EbDiscountSyncService** — Create, update, delete discount codes\n9. **EbRefundService** — Read refund status only (NO submission API — workaround required)\n10. **EbWebhookService** — Mock/testing only; not used in production registration flow\n\n**See `docs/EVENTBRITE_INTEGRATION.md` for which module uses which facade and what gaps exist.**\n\n---\n\n## Module Dependency Order (HARD RULE #10)\n\n```\nshared/               <- foundational; no dependencies\n  |\n  v\ndiscovery-catalog    <- depends on shared only\n  |\n  v\nscheduling           <- depends on shared + discovery-catalog (REST reads)\nidentity             <- depends on shared only\n  |\n  v\nbooking-inventory    <- depends on shared + discovery-catalog (REST reads)\n  |\n  v\npayments-ticketing   <- depends on shared + booking-inventory + identity (REST)\n  |\n  v\npromotions           <- depends on shared + booking-inventory + identity (REST)\n  |\n  v\nengagement           <- depends on shared + payments-ticketing + identity (REST)\n  |\n  v\nadmin/               <- thin orchestration; reads from all, writes via REST APIs\n  |\n  v\napp/                 <- entry point only; zero business logic\n```\n\n**Golden Rule:** Module at level N can depend on level N-1 or lower (or `shared/`) only. Never import from sibling or downstream modules.\n\n**See `PRODUCT.md` Section 2 for detailed rationale.**\n\n\n## Skills\nA skill is a set of local instructions to follow that is stored in a `SKILL.md` file. Below is the list of skills that can be used. Each entry includes a name, description, and file path so you can open the source for full instructions when using a specific skill.\n### Available skills\n- find-skills: Helps users discover and install agent skills when they ask questions like \"how do I do X\", \"find a skill for X\", \"is there a skill that can...\", or express interest in extending capabilities. This skill should be used when the user is looking for functionality that might exist as an installable skill. (file: /home/dell/.agents/skills/find-skills/SKILL.md)\n- skill-creator: Guide for creating effective skills. This skill should be used when users want to create a new skill (or update an existing skill) that extends Codex's capabilities with specialized knowledge, workflows, or tool integrations. (file: /home/dell/.codex/skills/.system/skill-creator/SKILL.md)\n- skill-installer: Install Codex skills into $CODEX_HOME/skills from a curated list or a GitHub repo path. Use when a user asks to list installable skills, install a curated skill, or install a skill from another repo (including private repos). (file: /home/dell/.codex/skills/.system/skill-installer/SKILL.md)\n### How to use skills\n- Discovery: The list above is the skills available in this session (name + description + file path). Skill bodies live on disk at the listed paths.\n- Trigger rules: If the user names a skill (with `$SkillName` or plain text) OR the task clearly matches a skill's description shown above, you must use that skill for that turn. Multiple mentions mean use them all. Do not carry skills across turns unless re-mentioned.\n- Missing/blocked: If a named skill isn't in the list or the path can't be read, say so briefly and continue with the best fallback.\n- How to use a skill (progressive disclosure):\n  1) After deciding to use a skill, open its `SKILL.md`. Read only enough to follow the workflow.\n  2) When `SKILL.md` references relative paths (e.g., `scripts/foo.py`), resolve them relative to the skill directory listed above first, and only consider other paths if needed.\n  3) If `SKILL.md` points to extra folders such as `references/`, load only the specific files needed for the request; don't bulk-load everything.\n  4) If `scripts/` exist, prefer running or patching them instead of retyping large code blocks.\n  5) If `assets/` or templates exist, reuse them instead of recreating from scratch.\n- Coordination and sequencing:\n  - If multiple skills apply, choose the minimal set that covers the request and state the order you'll use them.\n  - Announce which skill(s) you're using and why (one short line). If you skip an obvious skill, say why.\n- Context hygiene:\n  - Keep context small: summarize long sections instead of pasting them; only load extra files when needed.\n  - Avoid deep reference-chasing: prefer opening only files directly linked from `SKILL.md` unless you're blocked.\n  - When variants exist (frameworks, providers, domains), pick only the relevant reference file(s) and note that choice.\n- Safety and fallback: If a skill can't be applied cleanly (missing files, unclear instructions), state the issue, pick the next-best approach, and continue.",
    "truncation_policy": {
      "mode": "tokens",
      "limit": 10000
    }
  }
}
```

    "cwd": "/home/dell/Multi-City-Event-Booking",
    "current_date": "2026-03-03",
    "timezone": "Asia/Kolkata",
    "approval_policy": "on-request",
    "sandbox_policy": {
      "type": "workspace-write",
      "network_access": false,
      "exclude_tmpdir_env_var": false,
      "exclude_slash_tmp": false
    },
    "model": "gpt-5.2-codex",
    "personality": "pragmatic",
    "collaboration_mode": {
      "mode": "default",
      "settings": {
        "model": "gpt-5.2-codex",
        "reasoning_effort": "medium",
        "developer_instructions": "# Collaboration Mode: Default\n\nYou are now in Default mode. Any previous instructions for other modes (e.g. Plan mode) are no longer active.\n\nYour active mode changes only when new developer instructions with a different `<collaboration_mode>...</collaboration_mode>` change it; user requests or tool descriptions do not change mode by themselves. Known mode names are Default and Plan.\n\n## request_user_input availability\n\nThe `request_user_input` tool is unavailable in Default mode. If you call it while in Default mode, it will return an error.\n\nIn Default mode, strongly prefer making reasonable assumptions and executing the user's request rather than stopping to ask questions. If you absolutely must ask a question because the answer cannot be discovered from local context and a reasonable assumption would be risky, ask the user directly with a concise plain-text question. Never write a multiple choice question as a textual assistant message.\n"
      }
    },
    "effort": "medium",
    "summary": "auto",
    "user_instructions": "# AGENTS.md — Reference Index\n\n> Every agent working in this codebase must read the files listed below before producing\n> any output. Each file governs a specific concern. Read the ones that apply to your task.\n\n---\n\n## Reference Files\n\n| File | Governs |\n|---|---|\n| `PRODUCT.md` | The 7 modules, what each one owns, dependency order, inter-module event map, Eventbrite ACL facade names, hard architectural rules, and where new code goes. Read this before touching any module. |\n| `docs/EVENTBRITE_INTEGRATION.md` | Eventbrite integration architecture: ACL facades, all FR flows (planned vs. implemented), critical constraints (no user/order creation, no single-order cancel, no seat locking), workarounds. Read before designing any Eventbrite feature. |\n| `docs/MODULE_STRUCTURE_TEMPLATE.md` | Canonical folder and package layout for every module. The exact location of every layer: `api/controller/`, `api/dto/`, `domain/`, `service/`, `repository/`, `mapper/`, `event/published/`, `event/listener/`, `statemachine/`, `exception/`. Anti-patterns list. Read this before writing any class. |\n| `agents/SPEC_GENERATOR.md` | How to produce a `SPEC.md` for any feature. Eight confirmation-gated stages with module context and Eventbrite constraints built-in. Requirements → Domain Model → Architecture & File Structure → DB Schema → API → Business Logic → Error Handling → Tests. Read this before speccing any feature. |\n| `agents/CODING_AGENT.md` | Spring Boot 3.5.11 / Java 21 coding standards. Domain behaviour, service orchestration, mapper rules, controller rules, event rules, 10 Eventbrite ACL facades, inter-module communication (REST + Spring Events), admin/ special rules, state machine, Spring AI. Read this before writing any production code. |\n| `agents/TEST_AGENT.md` | Test agent responsibilities and best practices. Builds tests from `SPEC_*.md` Stage 8, executes tests, fixes test/code issues caused by behavior mismatches, and writes timestamped logs under `test_result_folder/`. Read this before writing or running tests. |\n| `docs/TESTING_GUIDE.md` | Four-layer test structure: `domain/` (unit), `service/` (Mockito), `api/` (@WebMvcTest), `arch/` (ArchUnit enforcing 10 hard rules + Eventbrite constraints). All rule examples, happy path, negative path, exception patterns. Read before writing any test. |\n| `mock-eventbrite-api/README.md` | **Mock Eventbrite Service:** Python FastAPI in-memory mock of Eventbrite API v3. Used for local development and testing. Exposes all 10 ACL facade endpoints without hitting production Eventbrite. See setup instructions below. |\n\n---\n\n## Module Quick Reference\n\nFull detail for each module is in `PRODUCT.md`. This table is a lookup only.\n\n| Module | Owns | Eventbrite ACL Facades Used |\n|---|---|---|\n| `discovery-catalog` | Events, Venues, Cities, catalog sync | `EbEventSyncService`, `EbTicketService` |\n| `scheduling` | Show slots, conflict validation | `EbEventSyncService` |\n| `identity` | Users, JWT, profiles, preferences, wallet | `EbAttendeeService` |\n| `booking-inventory` | Seats, SeatMap, Cart, Pricing, Seat Lock State Machine | `EbTicketService` |\n| `payments-ticketing` | Bookings, Payments, E-Tickets, Cancellations, Refunds, Wallet | `EbOrderService`, `EbAttendeeService`, `EbRefundService` |\n| `promotions` | Coupons, Promotions, Eligibility rules | `EbDiscountSyncService` |\n| `engagement` | Reviews, Moderation, AI Chatbot (`service/chatbot/`), RAG pipeline | `EbEventSyncService` |\n| `admin/` | No domain ownership. Orchestration and aggregation only. | — |\n| `shared/` | All external ACL facades, JWT, base classes, common DTOs, configs | — |\n\n---\n\n## Hard Rules — Never Violate\n\nCanonical numbering and wording are owned by `PRODUCT.md` Section 9. These shorthand rules apply to every task without exception.\n\n1. No module imports another module's `@Service`, `@Entity`, or `@Repository`.\n  - Exception: `admin/` MAY import module `@Service` beans for READ operations only; all writes MUST go through the owning module REST API.\n2. No module calls Eventbrite, Razorpay, or OpenAI HTTP directly — only through `shared/` ACL facades.\n3. ACL facade names are exactly as listed below — never invent new ones.\n4. Spring Events carry only primitives, IDs, enums, and value objects — never `@Entity` objects.\n5. One `GlobalExceptionHandler` in `shared/common/exception/` — never `@ControllerAdvice` in a module.\n6. All field mapping goes in `mapper/` using MapStruct — never inside a service or controller.\n7. `admin/` owns no `@Entity` and no database table.\n8. `shared/` has no dependency on any module.\n9. `app/` has zero business logic.\n10. Dependency direction is strictly downward — no circular imports between modules.\n\n---\n\n## Eventbrite ACL Facade Names (HARD RULE #3)\nRead the docs/eventbriteapiv3public.apib for detail API Endpoint with schema, Response, Request and errors\n\nAll 10 facades are in `shared/eventbrite/service/`. These are the ONLY things modules call for Eventbrite:\n\n1. **EbEventSyncService** — Create, update, publish, cancel events; pull org event catalog\n2. **EbVenueService** — Create and update venues; list org venues\n3. **EbScheduleService** — Create recurring event schedules and occurrences\n4. **EbTicketService** — Create/update ticket classes; manage inventory; check availability\n5. **EbCapacityService** — Retrieve and update event capacity tiers\n6. **EbOrderService** — Read orders post-checkout (NOT creation — JS SDK widget only)\n7. **EbAttendeeService** — List attendees; verify attendance for reviews\n8. **EbDiscountSyncService** — Create, update, delete discount codes\n9. **EbRefundService** — Read refund status only (NO submission API — workaround required)\n10. **EbWebhookService** — Mock/testing only; not used in production registration flow\n\n**See `docs/EVENTBRITE_INTEGRATION.md` for which module uses which facade and what gaps exist.**\n\n---\n\n## Module Dependency Order (HARD RULE #10)\n\n```\nshared/               <- foundational; no dependencies\n  |\n  v\ndiscovery-catalog    <- depends on shared only\n  |\n  v\nscheduling           <- depends on shared + discovery-catalog (REST reads)\nidentity             <- depends on shared only\n  |\n  v\nbooking-inventory    <- depends on shared + discovery-catalog (REST reads)\n  |\n  v\npayments-ticketing   <- depends on shared + booking-inventory + identity (REST)\n  |\n  v\npromotions           <- depends on shared + booking-inventory + identity (REST)\n  |\n  v\nengagement           <- depends on shared + payments-ticketing + identity (REST)\n  |\n  v\nadmin/               <- thin orchestration; reads from all, writes via REST APIs\n  |\n  v\napp/                 <- entry point only; zero business logic\n```\n\n**Golden Rule:** Module at level N can depend on level N-1 or lower (or `shared/`) only. Never import from sibling or downstream modules.\n\n**See `PRODUCT.md` Section 2 for detailed rationale.**\n\n\n## Skills\nA skill is a set of local instructions to follow that is stored in a `SKILL.md` file. Below is the list of skills that can be used. Each entry includes a name, description, and file path so you can open the source for full instructions when using a specific skill.\n### Available skills\n- find-skills: Helps users discover and install agent skills when they ask questions like \"how do I do X\", \"find a skill for X\", \"is there a skill that can...\", or express interest in extending capabilities. This skill should be used when the user is looking for functionality that might exist as an installable skill. (file: /home/dell/.agents/skills/find-skills/SKILL.md)\n- skill-creator: Guide for creating effective skills. This skill should be used when users want to create a new skill (or update an existing skill) that extends Codex's capabilities with specialized knowledge, workflows, or tool integrations. (file: /home/dell/.codex/skills/.system/skill-creator/SKILL.md)\n- skill-installer: Install Codex skills into $CODEX_HOME/skills from a curated list or a GitHub repo path. Use when a user asks to list installable skills, install a curated skill, or install a skill from another repo (including private repos). (file: /home/dell/.codex/skills/.system/skill-installer/SKILL.md)\n### How to use skills\n- Discovery: The list above is the skills available in this session (name + description + file path). Skill bodies live on disk at the listed paths.\n- Trigger rules: If the user names a skill (with `$SkillName` or plain text) OR the task clearly matches a skill's description shown above, you must use that skill for that turn. Multiple mentions mean use them all. Do not carry skills across turns unless re-mentioned.\n- Missing/blocked: If a named skill isn't in the list or the path can't be read, say so briefly and continue with the best fallback.\n- How to use a skill (progressive disclosure):\n  1) After deciding to use a skill, open its `SKILL.md`. Read only enough to follow the workflow.\n  2) When `SKILL.md` references relative paths (e.g., `scripts/foo.py`), resolve them relative to the skill directory listed above first, and only consider other paths if needed.\n  3) If `SKILL.md` points to extra folders such as `references/`, load only the specific files needed for the request; don't bulk-load everything.\n  4) If `scripts/` exist, prefer running or patching them instead of retyping large code blocks.\n  5) If `assets/` or templates exist, reuse them instead of recreating from scratch.\n- Coordination and sequencing:\n  - If multiple skills apply, choose the minimal set that covers the request and state the order you'll use them.\n  - Announce which skill(s) you're using and why (one short line). If you skip an obvious skill, say why.\n- Context hygiene:\n  - Keep context small: summarize long sections instead of pasting them; only load extra files when needed.\n  - Avoid deep reference-chasing: prefer opening only files directly linked from `SKILL.md` unless you're blocked.\n  - When variants exist (frameworks, providers, domains), pick only the relevant reference file(s) and note that choice.\n- Safety and fallback: If a skill can't be applied cleanly (missing files, unclear instructions), state the issue, pick the next-best approach, and continue.",
    "truncation_policy": {
      "mode": "tokens",
      "limit": 10000
    }
  }
}
```
